papers.title: SenseNova-U1: Unifying Multimodal Understanding and Generation with NEO-unify Architecture
papers.abstract: Abstract Unified vision-language models treat understanding and generation as integrated processes rather than separate tasks, demonstrating strong performance across multiple multimodal capabilities including image synthesis and action reasoning.  AI-generated summary Recent large vision-language models (VLMs) remain fundamentally constrained by a persistent dichotomy: understanding and generation are treated as distinct problems, leading to fragmented architectures, cascaded pipelines, and misaligned representation spaces. We argue that this divide is not merely an engineering artifact, but a structural limitation that hinders the emergence of native multimodal intelligence. Hence, we introduce SenseNova-U1, a native unified multimodal paradigm built upon NEO-unify, in which understanding and generation evolve as synergistic views of a single underlying process. We launch two native unified variants, SenseNova-U1-8B-MoT and SenseNova-U1-A3B-MoT, built on dense (8B) and mixture-of-experts (30B-A3B) understanding baselines, respectively. Designed from first principles, they rival top-tier understanding-only VLMs across text understanding, vision-language perception, knowledge reasoning, agentic decision-making, and spatial intelligence. Meanwhile, they deliver strong semantic consistency and visual fidelity, excelling in conventional or knowledge-intensive any-to-image (X2I) synthesis, complex text-rich infographic generation, and interleaved vision-language generation, with or without think patterns. Beyond performance, we show detailed model design, data preprocessing, pre-/post-training, and inference strategies to support community research. Last but not least, preliminary evidence demonstrates that our models extend beyond perception and generation, performing strongly in vision-language-action (VLA) and world model (WM) scenarios. This points toward a broader roadmap where models do not translate between modalities, but think and act across them in a native manner. Multimodal AI is no longer about connecting separate systems, but about building a unified one and trusting the necessary capabilities to emerge from within.

ru.title: Единая мультимодальная система: от перевода модальностей к синергическому мышлению
ru.desc: В статье представлена единая архитектура видео-языковой модели SenseNova-U1, которая объединяет понимание и генерацию изображений как синергические процессы вместо отдельных задач. Модель построена на основе NEO-unify парадигмы в двух вариантах: с плотной архитектурой (8B параметров) и с механизмом смеси экспертов (30B параметров). Система демонстрирует сильные результаты как в классических задачах понимания визуально-языковой информации и рассуждений, так и в синтезе изображений, генерации инфографики и решении агентских задач. Авторы показывают, что единый подход к обработке мультимодальных данных позволяет моделям естественным образом мыслить и действовать в разных модальностях без прямого трансляции между ними.

en.title: Unifying Understanding and Generation for Multimodal Intelligence
en.desc: This paper introduces SenseNova-U1, a unified vision-language model that integrates understanding and generation into a single process, overcoming the limitations of traditional models that treat these tasks separately. By utilizing a novel architecture called NEO-unify, the model achieves strong performance in various multimodal tasks, including image synthesis and reasoning. The authors present two variants of the model, demonstrating its capabilities in text understanding, visual perception, and decision-making, while maintaining high semantic consistency and visual quality. The research emphasizes the importance of a unified approach in developing advanced multimodal AI that can think and act across different modalities without relying on separate systems.

zh.title: 统一多模态智能的未来
zh.desc: 这篇论文介绍了一种新的统一视觉-语言模型SenseNova-U1，它将理解和生成视为一个整体过程，而不是分开的任务。这种方法克服了传统模型在理解和生成之间的结构性限制，促进了多模态智能的自然发展。SenseNova-U1在文本理解、视觉-语言感知和知识推理等多个领域表现出色，并在图像合成和复杂信息图生成中展现了强大的语义一致性和视觉保真度。该模型的设计和训练策略为社区研究提供了详细的支持，展示了多模态AI的未来发展方向。

papers.title: δ-mem: Efficient Online Memory for Large Language Models
papers.abstract: Abstract A lightweight memory mechanism called δ-mem enhances large language models by augmenting a frozen attention backbone with a compact associative memory state that provides low-rank corrections to attention computations.  AI-generated summary Large language models increasingly need to accumulate and reuse historical information in long-term assistants and agent systems. Simply expanding the context window is costly and often fails to ensure effective context utilization. We propose δ-mem, a lightweight memory mechanism that augments a frozen full-attention backbone with a compact online state of associative memory. δ-mem compresses past information into a fixed-size state matrix updated by delta-rule learning, and uses its readout to generate low-rank corrections to the backbone's attention computation during generation. With only an 8times8 online memory state, δ-mem improves the average score to 1.10times that of the frozen backbone and 1.15times that of the strongest non-δ-mem memory baseline. It achieves larger gains on memory-heavy benchmarks, reaching 1.31times on MemoryAgentBench and 1.20times on LoCoMo, while largely preserving general capabilities. These results show that effective memory can be realized through a compact online state directly coupled with attention computation, without full fine-tuning, backbone replacement, or explicit context extension.

The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology (Guangzhou)

ru.title: Компактная память для больших языковых моделей без переобучения
ru.desc: В работе представлен δ-mem — лёгкий механизм памяти, который дополняет замороженную сеть внимания компактным состоянием ассоциативной памяти. Механизм сжимает историческую информацию в матрицу фиксированного размера (8×8), которая обновляется с помощью дельта-обучения, и использует её для генерации низкоранговых коррекций к вычислениям внимания. δ-mem не требует дополнительной настройки всей модели, замены архитектуры или расширения контекстного окна. Эксперименты показывают улучшение производительности в 1.10–1.31 раза на задачах, требующих долгосрочной памяти, при сохранении основных способностей модели.

en.title: Enhancing Language Models with Compact Memory Mechanism
en.desc: The paper introduces δ-mem, a lightweight memory mechanism designed to enhance large language models by integrating a compact associative memory state with a frozen attention backbone. This mechanism allows the model to efficiently accumulate and utilize historical information without the need for costly context window expansions. δ-mem employs delta-rule learning to update a fixed-size state matrix, which provides low-rank corrections to the attention computations during text generation. The results demonstrate that δ-mem significantly improves performance on memory-intensive tasks while maintaining the model's overall capabilities.

zh.title: 轻量级记忆机制δ-mem提升语言模型性能
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种轻量级的记忆机制δ-mem，旨在增强大型语言模型的性能。它通过在固定的注意力骨干网络上增加一个紧凑的关联记忆状态，提供低秩修正来优化注意力计算。δ-mem将过去的信息压缩成一个固定大小的状态矩阵，并通过增量学习进行更新，从而在生成过程中对注意力计算进行修正。实验结果表明，δ-mem在内存密集型基准测试中表现出显著的性能提升，同时保持了模型的通用能力。

papers.title: MemPrivacy: Privacy-Preserving Personalized Memory Management for Edge-Cloud Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract MemPrivacy enables privacy-preserving personalized memory in edge-cloud environments by using type-aware placeholders to protect sensitive data while maintaining semantic integrity for effective memory operations.  AI-generated summary As LLM-powered agents are increasingly deployed in edge-cloud environments, personalized memory has become a key enabler of long-term adaptation and user-centric interaction. However, cloud-assisted memory management exposes sensitive user information, while existing privacy protection methods typically rely on aggressive masking that removes task-relevant semantics and consequently degrades memory utility and personalization quality. To address this challenge, We propose MemPrivacy, which identifies privacy-sensitive spans on edge devices, replaces them with semantically structured type-aware placeholders for cloud-side memory processing, and restores the original values locally when needed. By decoupling privacy protection from semantic destruction, MemPrivacy minimizes sensitive data exposure while retaining the information required for effective memory formation and retrieval. We also construct MemPrivacy-Bench for systematic evaluation, a dataset covering 200 users and over 52k privacy instances, and introduce a four-level privacy taxonomy for configurable protection policies. Experiments show that MemPrivacy achieves strong performance in privacy information extraction, substantially surpassing strong general-purpose models such as GPT-5.2 and Gemini-3.1-Pro, while also reducing inference latency. Across multiple widely used memory systems, MemPrivacy limits utility loss to within 1.6%, outperforming baseline masking strategies. Overall, MemPrivacy offers an effective balance between privacy protection and personalized memory utility for edge-cloud agents, enabling secure, practical, and user-transparent deployment.

ru.title: Защита приватности без потери смысла в памяти агентов
ru.desc: MemPrivacy — это система для защиты приватных данных в персонализированной памяти LLM-агентов, развёрнутых в гибридных окружениях edge-cloud. Вместо агрессивного маскирования, которое разрушает семантику и снижает полезность памяти, метод заменяет чувствительные данные на типизированные плейсхолдеры, сохраняя семантическую целостность. Восстановление исходных значений происходит локально на edge-устройстве, что минимизирует утечку приватной информации в облако. Экспериментальная оценка показывает, что MemPrivacy достигает баланса между защитой данных и качеством персонализации с потерей полезности менее 1,6%.

en.title: Balancing Privacy and Memory Utility in Edge-Cloud Systems
en.desc: MemPrivacy is a novel approach designed to protect sensitive user data in edge-cloud environments while still allowing for personalized memory management. It uses type-aware placeholders to replace privacy-sensitive information, ensuring that the semantic meaning is preserved for effective memory operations. This method allows for the retrieval of original data locally, minimizing exposure of sensitive information without sacrificing memory utility. The proposed system has been evaluated using a comprehensive dataset and has shown to outperform existing privacy protection methods in both privacy extraction and memory performance.

zh.title: 隐私保护与个性化记忆的完美平衡
zh.desc: MemPrivacy是一种在边缘云环境中实现隐私保护的个性化记忆的方法。它通过使用类型感知的占位符来保护敏感数据，同时保持语义完整性，以便有效地进行记忆操作。该方法能够在边缘设备上识别隐私敏感的内容，并在云端处理时用占位符替代，必要时再在本地恢复原始值。实验表明，MemPrivacy在隐私信息提取方面表现优异，同时在多个记忆系统中保持了较低的效用损失。

papers.title: RubricEM: Meta-RL with Rubric-guided Policy Decomposition beyond Verifiable Rewards
papers.abstract: Abstract Deep research agents trained using RubricEM framework demonstrate superior performance on long-form research tasks through rubric-guided reinforcement learning with stage-aware planning and reflection-based meta-policy evolution.  AI-generated summary Training deep research agents, namely systems that plan, search, evaluate evidence, and synthesize long-form reports, pushes reinforcement learning beyond the regime of verifiable rewards. Their outputs lack ground-truth answers, their trajectories span many tool-augmented decisions, and standard post-training offers little mechanism for turning past attempts into reusable experience. In this work, we argue that rubrics should serve not merely as final-answer evaluators, but as the shared interface that structures policy execution, judge feedback, and agent memory. Based on this view, we introduce RubricEM, a rubric-guided reinforcement learning framework that combines stagewise policy decomposition with reflection-based meta-policy evolution. RubricEM first makes research trajectories stage-aware by conditioning planning, evidence gathering, review, and synthesis on self-generated rubrics. It then assigns credit with Stage-Structured GRPO, which uses stagewise rubric judgments to provide denser semantic feedback for long-horizon optimization. In parallel, RubricEM trains a shared-backbone reflection meta-policy that distills judged trajectories into reusable rubric-grounded guidance for future attempts. The resulting RubricEM-8B achieves strong performance across four long-form research benchmarks, outperforming comparable open models and approaching proprietary deep-research systems. Beyond final performance, we perform thorough analyses to understand the key ingredients of RubricEM.

ru.title: Рубрики как универсальный интерфейс для управления траекториями исследовательских агентов
ru.desc: В работе представлена RubricEM — фреймворк для обучения глубоких исследовательских агентов на основе обучения с подкреплением, управляемого рубриками. Ключевая идея заключается в том, что рубрики должны структурировать не только финальную оценку, но и планирование, сбор доказательств и память агента на протяжении всей траектории. Фреймворк комбинирует разбиение политики по этапам с эволюцией мета-политики через рефлексию, обеспечивая более плотную семантическую обратную связь для оптимизации долгосрочных задач. Модель RubricEM-8B достигает сильных результатов на бенчмарках для создания длинных исследовательских текстов, превосходя открытые модели и приближаясь к коммерческим системам.

en.title: Empowering Research Agents with Rubric-Guided Learning
en.desc: This paper presents RubricEM, a framework for training deep research agents that excel in long-form research tasks using reinforcement learning. It emphasizes the importance of rubrics not just for evaluating final outputs, but as a guiding structure for the entire research process, including planning and evidence synthesis. The framework incorporates stage-aware planning and reflection-based meta-policy evolution to enhance learning from past experiences. The results show that RubricEM-8B significantly outperforms other models on long-form research benchmarks, demonstrating its effectiveness in optimizing complex research tasks.

zh.title: RubricEM：提升深度研究代理的强化学习框架
zh.desc: 本论文介绍了一种名为RubricEM的框架，用于训练深度研究代理，以提高其在长篇研究任务中的表现。该框架通过基于评分标准的强化学习，结合阶段意识的规划和反思式的元策略演化，来优化研究过程。RubricEM不仅将评分标准作为最终答案的评估工具，还作为结构化政策执行、反馈评估和代理记忆的共享接口。实验结果表明，RubricEM-8B在多个长篇研究基准测试中表现优异，超越了类似的开放模型，接近专有的深度研究系统。

papers.title: World Action Models: The Next Frontier in Embodied AI
papers.abstract: Abstract World Action Models unify predictive state modeling with action generation for embodied policy learning, forming a cohesive framework for understanding environment dynamics and action prediction.  AI-generated summary Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have achieved strong semantic generalization for embodied policy learning, yet they learn reactive observation-to-action mappings without explicitly modeling how the physical world evolves under intervention. A growing body of work addresses this limitation by integrating world models, predictive models of environment dynamics, into the action generation pipeline. We term this emerging paradigm World Action Models (WAMs): embodied foundation models that unify predictive state modeling with action generation, targeting a joint distribution over future states and actions rather than actions alone. However, the literature remains fragmented across architectures, learning objectives, and application scenarios, lacking a unified conceptual framework. We formally define WAMs and disambiguate them from related concepts, and trace the foundations and early integration of VLA and world model research that gave rise to this paradigm. We organize existing methods into a structured taxonomy of Cascaded and Joint WAMs, with further subdivision by generation modality, conditioning mechanism, and action decoding strategy. We systematically analyze the data ecosystem fueling WAMs development, spanning robot teleoperation, portable human demonstrations, simulation, and internet-scale egocentric video, and synthesize emerging evaluation protocols organized around visual fidelity, physical commonsense, and action plausibility. Overall, this survey provides the first systematic account of the WAMs landscape, clarifies key architectural paradigms and their trade-offs, and identifies open challenges and future opportunities for this rapidly evolving field.

ru.title: Объединяя предсказание мира и генерацию действий в единую модель
ru.desc: В статье предложена унифицированная концепция World Action Models (WAM) — моделей, которые объединяют предсказание динамики окружающей среды с генерацией действий для обучения политик роботов. Авторы систематизируют существующие подходы в виде таксономии, разделяя методы на каскадные и совместные архитектуры в зависимости от модальности генерации и стратегии декодирования действий. Исследование охватывает экосистему данных для обучения WAM, включая телеоперацию, человеческие демонстрации, симуляции и видео с егоцентрической точки зрения. Работа определяет ключевые метрики оценки на основе визуальной точности, физического здравого смысла и правдоподобности действий, предоставляя первый систематический обзор этой быстро развивающейся области.

en.title: Unifying Prediction and Action in AI with World Action Models
en.desc: World Action Models (WAMs) combine predictive state modeling with action generation to enhance embodied policy learning. This framework allows AI systems to not only predict future states but also to understand how actions influence the environment. The paper categorizes existing WAM methods into Cascaded and Joint models, detailing their architectures and learning objectives. It also highlights the diverse data sources that support WAM development and outlines evaluation protocols to assess their performance in real-world scenarios.

zh.title: 统一预测与动作生成的世界动作模型
zh.desc: 世界动作模型（WAMs）将预测状态建模与动作生成结合起来，为体态政策学习提供了一个统一的框架。尽管视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型在语义泛化方面表现出色，但它们在学习反应性观察到动作的映射时，未能明确建模物理世界在干预下的演变。WAMs通过将环境动态的预测模型整合到动作生成流程中，解决了这一局限性。本文系统分析了WAMs的发展现状，明确了关键架构范式及其权衡，并识别了该快速发展的领域中的开放挑战和未来机会。

papers.title: Beyond the Last Layer: Multi-Layer Representation Fusion for Visual Tokenization
papers.abstract: Abstract DRoRAE enhances visual representation by fusing multi-layer features from pretrained vision encoders through adaptive routing and incremental correction, improving reconstruction and generation quality.  AI-generated summary Representation autoencoders that reuse frozen pretrained vision encoders as visual tokenizers have achieved strong reconstruction and generation quality. However, existing methods universally extract features from only the last encoder layer, discarding the rich hierarchical information distributed across intermediate layers. We show that low-level visual details survive in the last layer merely as attenuated residuals after multiple layers of semantic abstraction, and that explicitly fusing multi-layer features can substantially recover this lost information. We propose DRoRAE (Depth-Routed Representation AutoEncoder), a lightweight fusion module that adaptively aggregates all encoder layers via energy-constrained routing and incremental correction, producing an enriched latent compatible with a frozen pretrained decoder. A three-phase decoupled training strategy first learns the fusion under the implicit distributional constraint of the frozen decoder, then fine-tunes the decoder to fully exploit the enriched representation. On ImageNet-256, DRoRAE reduces rFID from 0.57 to 0.29 and improves generation FID from 1.74 to 1.65 (with AutoGuidance), with gains also transferring to text-to-image synthesis. Furthermore, we uncover a log-linear scaling law (R^2{=}0.86) between fusion capacity and reconstruction quality, identifying representation richness as a new, predictably scalable dimension for visual tokenizers analogous to vocabulary size in NLP.

ru.title: Многослойное слияние признаков для превосходной реконструкции изображений
ru.desc: Авторы предлагают DRoRAE — метод для улучшения визуальных представлений путём адаптивного объединения признаков со всех слоёв замороженного энкодера, а не только последнего слоя. Система использует маршрутизацию с энергетическими ограничениями и инкрементальную коррекцию для создания обогащённого латентного пространства, совместимого с предобученным декодером. Трёхфазная стратегия обучения позволяет сначала обучить модуль слияния под неявным ограничением декодера, а затем тонко настроить декодер для полного использования обогащённого представления. Исследование обнаруживает логарифмически-линейный закон масштабирования между ёмкостью слияния и качеством реконструкции, что открывает новое масштабируемое измерение для улучшения визуальных токенизаторов.

en.title: Enhancing Visual Representation through Multi-Layer Feature Fusion
en.desc: The paper introduces DRoRAE, a novel approach that enhances visual representation by combining features from multiple layers of pretrained vision encoders. Traditional methods typically rely on features from only the last layer, which can lead to a loss of important visual details. DRoRAE employs a lightweight fusion module that adaptively integrates features from all layers, improving the quality of image reconstruction and generation. The authors demonstrate that this method significantly reduces reconstruction error and enhances performance in tasks like text-to-image synthesis, while also revealing a relationship between fusion capacity and reconstruction quality.

zh.title: DRoRAE：融合多层特征提升视觉表示
zh.desc: DRoRAE是一种增强视觉表示的方法，通过自适应路由和增量修正融合来自预训练视觉编码器的多层特征，从而提高重建和生成质量。现有方法通常只提取最后一层编码器的特征，忽略了中间层分布的丰富层次信息。研究表明，低级视觉细节在最后一层仅作为减弱的残差存在，而显式融合多层特征可以显著恢复这些丢失的信息。DRoRAE通过能量约束路由和增量修正，适应性地聚合所有编码器层，生成与冻结的预训练解码器兼容的丰富潜在表示。

papers.title: CausalCine: Real-Time Autoregressive Generation for Multi-Shot Video Narratives
papers.abstract: Abstract CausalCine enables interactive, multi-shot video generation by addressing limitations of autoregressive models through causal modeling, dynamic memory routing, and real-time distillation techniques.  AI-generated summary Autoregressive video generation aims at real-time, open-ended synthesis. Yet, cinematic storytelling is not merely the endless extension of a single scene; it requires progressing through evolving events, viewpoint shifts, and discrete shot boundaries. Existing autoregressive models often struggle in this setting. Trained primarily for short-horizon continuation, they treat long sequences as extended single shots, inevitably suffering from motion stagnation and semantic drift during long rollouts. To bridge this gap, we introduce CausalCine, an interactive autoregressive framework that transforms multi-shot video generation into an online directing process. CausalCine generates causally across shot changes, accepts dynamic prompts on the fly, and reuses context without regenerating previous shots. To achieve this, we first train a causal base model on native multi-shot sequences to learn complex shot transitions prior to acceleration. We then propose Content-Aware Memory Routing (CAMR), which dynamically retrieves historical KV entries according to attention-based relevance scores rather than temporal proximity, preserving cross-shot coherence under bounded active memory. Finally, we distill the causal base model into a few-step generator for real-time interactive generation. Extensive experiments demonstrate that CausalCine significantly outperforms autoregressive baselines and approaches the capability of bidirectional models while unlocking the streaming interactivity of causal generation. Demo available at https://yihao-meng.github.io/CausalCine/

ru.title: Интерактивная режиссура видео через причинное моделирование с динамической маршрутизацией памяти
ru.desc: CausalCine решает проблему долгосрочной генерации видео, используя причинное моделирование вместо традиционного авторегрессивного подхода. Модель обучается на многокадровых последовательностях и использует механизм Content-Aware Memory Routing для динамического выбора релевантного контекста на основе оценок внимания, а не временной близости. Система поддерживает интерактивное управление с возможностью динамических подсказок в реальном времени без переконструирования предыдущих кадров. Дистилляция причинной базовой модели позволяет достичь генерации видео в реальном времени при сохранении когерентности между переходами кадров.

en.title: Revolutionizing Video Generation with CausalCine
en.desc: CausalCine is a novel framework for generating interactive, multi-shot videos that overcomes the limitations of traditional autoregressive models. It focuses on cinematic storytelling, which requires managing transitions between different scenes and viewpoints rather than just extending a single shot. By employing techniques like Content-Aware Memory Routing, CausalCine maintains coherence across shots and allows for dynamic prompts during video generation. This approach enables real-time, interactive video creation while significantly improving performance compared to existing autoregressive methods.

zh.title: CausalCine：交互式多镜头视频生成的新突破
zh.desc: CausalCine 是一种交互式的多镜头视频生成框架，旨在解决自回归模型的局限性。它通过因果建模、动态记忆路由和实时蒸馏技术，实现了更复杂的镜头转换和实时生成。与传统的自回归模型不同，CausalCine 能够在镜头变化时生成因果内容，并根据动态提示进行调整。实验表明，CausalCine 在性能上显著优于自回归基线，并接近双向模型的能力，同时实现了因果生成的流媒体交互性。

papers.title: L2P: Unlocking Latent Potential for Pixel Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract Latent-to-Pixel transfer paradigm efficiently leverages pre-trained latent diffusion models to create pixel-space models with minimal training overhead and high-resolution generation capabilities.  AI-generated summary Pixel diffusion models have recently regained attention for visual generation. However, training advanced pixel-space models from scratch demands prohibitive computational and data resources. To address this, we propose the Latent-to-Pixel (L2P) transfer paradigm, an efficient framework that directly harnesses the rich knowledge of pre-trained LDMs to build powerful pixel-space models. Specifically, L2P discards the VAE in favor of large-patch tokenization and freezes the source LDM's intermediate layers, exclusively training shallow layers to learn the latent-to-pixel transformation. By utilizing LDM-generated synthetic images as the sole training corpus, L2P fits an already smooth data manifold, enabling rapid convergence with zero real-data collection. This strategy allows L2P to seamlessly migrate massive latent priors to the pixel space using only 8 GPUs. Furthermore, eliminating the VAE memory bottleneck unlocks native 4K ultra-high resolution generation. Extensive experiments across mainstream LDM architectures show that L2P incurs negligible training overhead, yet performs on par with the source LDM on DPG-Bench and reaches 93% performance on GenEval.

ru.title: От латентного пространства к пиксельному: эффективный перенос знаний диффузионных моделей
ru.desc: В статье предложена парадигма Latent-to-Pixel (L2P), которая эффективно переносит знания предварительно обученных латентных диффузионных моделей в пиксельное пространство с минимальными вычислительными затратами. Метод заменяет VAE на крупнозерновую токенизацию и замораживает промежуточные слои исходной модели, обучая только поверхностные слои преобразованию из латентного пространства в пиксельное. L2P использует синтетические изображения, сгенерированные диффузионной моделью, для обучения, что позволяет избежать сбора реальных данных и достичь быстрой сходимости. Благодаря устранению узкого места памяти VAE, метод поддерживает генерацию ультравысокого разрешения до 4K.

en.title: Efficiently Transforming Latent Knowledge to High-Resolution Pixels
en.desc: The paper introduces the Latent-to-Pixel (L2P) transfer paradigm, which efficiently transforms pre-trained latent diffusion models (LDMs) into pixel-space models. This approach minimizes the need for extensive training by utilizing synthetic images generated from LDMs, allowing for rapid convergence without requiring real data. By freezing the intermediate layers of the source LDM and only training shallow layers, L2P effectively learns the transformation from latent to pixel space. The method achieves high-resolution image generation while significantly reducing computational overhead, demonstrating competitive performance with existing models.

zh.title: 高效的潜在到像素转移，快速生成高分辨率图像
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种名为潜在到像素（L2P）转移范式的高效框架，旨在利用预训练的潜在扩散模型（LDM）来构建强大的像素空间模型。L2P通过丢弃变分自编码器（VAE），采用大块标记化，并冻结源LDM的中间层，仅训练浅层以学习潜在到像素的转换。该方法使用LDM生成的合成图像作为唯一的训练数据，能够快速收敛，且无需真实数据收集。实验表明，L2P在训练开销上几乎可以忽略不计，同时在多个主流LDM架构上表现出色，达到了93%的性能。

papers.title: ToolCUA: Towards Optimal GUI-Tool Path Orchestration for Computer Use Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract ToolCUA is an end-to-end agent that learns optimal GUI-tool path selection through staged training, achieving superior performance in hybrid action space environments.  AI-generated summary Computer Use Agents (CUAs) can act through both atomic GUI actions, such as click and type, and high-level tool calls, such as API-based file operations, but this hybrid action space often leaves them uncertain about when to continue with GUI actions or switch to tools, leading to suboptimal execution paths. This difficulty stems from the scarcity of high-quality interleaved GUI-Tool trajectories, the cost and brittleness of collecting real tool trajectories, and the lack of trajectory-level supervision for GUI-Tool path selection. In this paper, we propose ToolCUA, an end-to-end agent designed to learn optimal GUI-Tool path selection through a staged training paradigm. We first introduce an Interleaved GUI-Tool Trajectory Scaling Pipeline that repurposes abundant static GUI trajectories and synthesizes a grounded tool library, enabling diverse GUI-Tool trajectories without manual engineering or real tool-trajectory collection. We then perform Tool-Bootstrapped GUI RFT, combining warmup SFT with single-turn RL to improve decisions at critical GUI-Tool switching points. Finally, we optimize ToolCUA with Online Agentic RL in a high-fidelity GUI-Tool environment, guided by a Tool-Efficient Path Reward that encourages appropriate tool use and shorter execution paths. Experiments on OSWorld-MCP show that ToolCUA achieves 46.85% accuracy, a relative improvement of approximately 66% over the baseline, establishing a new state of the art among models of comparable scale. It also improves by 3.9% over GUI-only settings, demonstrating effective GUI-Tool orchestration. The results further suggest that training in a hybrid action space is a promising paradigm for real-world digital agents. Open-sourced here: https://x-plug.github.io/ToolCUA/

ru.title: Гибридное действие: агент, научившийся выбирать между кликами и вызовами
ru.desc: ToolCUA — это агент, который учится выбирать оптимальный путь между GUI-действиями и вызовами инструментов через трёхэтапное обучение. Авторы предлагают конвейер для масштабирования траекторий, синтезирующий библиотеку инструментов на основе существующих GUI-данных без необходимости в дорогостоящем сборе реальных траекторий. Затем они применяют комбинированное обучение с подкреплением, используя награду за эффективность инструментов, которая поощряет правильное их использование и более короткие пути выполнения. Модель достигает 46.85% точности на бенчмарке OSWorld-MCP, что на 66% лучше базовой модели и демонстрирует эффективную оркестрацию GUI и инструментов.

en.title: Optimizing GUI-Tool Path Selection with ToolCUA
en.desc: ToolCUA is an advanced agent that optimizes the selection of paths between graphical user interface (GUI) actions and tool operations through a structured training approach. It addresses the challenges faced by Computer Use Agents (CUAs) in navigating a hybrid action space, where decisions on whether to perform GUI actions or switch to tool calls can be unclear. By utilizing a novel Interleaved GUI-Tool Trajectory Scaling Pipeline, ToolCUA generates diverse training data without the need for extensive manual input or real-world data collection. The agent's performance is further enhanced through a combination of supervised fine-tuning and reinforcement learning, leading to significant improvements in execution accuracy and efficiency in complex environments.

zh.title: ToolCUA：优化 GUI-工具路径选择的智能体
zh.desc: ToolCUA 是一个端到端的智能体，通过分阶段训练学习最佳的 GUI-工具路径选择，能够在混合动作空间环境中实现优越的性能。该智能体可以通过基本的 GUI 操作（如点击和输入）以及高级工具调用（如基于 API 的文件操作）进行操作，但在何时继续 GUI 操作或切换到工具方面常常存在不确定性，导致执行路径不理想。为了解决这一问题，ToolCUA 引入了一种交错的 GUI-工具轨迹缩放管道，利用丰富的静态 GUI 轨迹合成工具库，从而无需手动工程或真实工具轨迹收集。实验结果表明，ToolCUA 在 OSWorld-MCP 上达到了 46.85% 的准确率，相较于基线模型提高了约 66%，并在 GUI-工具协同方面表现出色。

papers.title: Do Enterprise Systems Need Learned World Models? The Importance of Context to Infer Dynamics
papers.abstract: Abstract Enterprise discovery agents that read system configuration at runtime outperform traditional world models in configurable environments where dynamics change over time.  AI-generated summary World models enable agents to anticipate the effects of their actions by internalizing environment dynamics. In enterprise systems, however, these dynamics are often defined by tenant-specific business logic that varies across deployments and evolves over time, making models trained on historical transitions brittle under deployment shift. We ask a question the world-models literature has not addressed: when the rules can be read at inference time, does an agent still need to learn them? We argue, and demonstrate empirically, that in settings where transition dynamics are configurable and readable, runtime discovery complements offline training by grounding predictions in the active system instance. We propose enterprise discovery agents, which recover relevant transition dynamics at runtime by reading the system's configuration rather than relying solely on internalized representations. We introduce CascadeBench, a reasoning-focused benchmark for enterprise cascade prediction that adopts the evaluation methodology of World of Workflows on diverse synthetic environments, and use it together with deployment-shift evaluation to show that offline-trained world models can perform well in-distribution but degrade as dynamics change, whereas discovery-based agents are more robust under shift by grounding their predictions in the current instance. Our findings suggest that, in configurable enterprise environments, agents should not rely solely on fixed internalized dynamics, but should incorporate mechanisms for discovering relevant transition logic at runtime.

ru.title: Читай конфигурацию вместо запоминания правил
ru.desc: В статье предложен новый подход к агентам в корпоративных системах, которые читают конфигурацию окружения во время работы вместо использования предварительно обученных моделей мира. Авторы показывают, что когда правила динамики системы доступны во время инференса, агент может достичь лучших результатов, опираясь на актуальную конфигурацию, а не на выученные представления. Представлен новый бенчмарк CascadeBench для оценки предсказания каскадов в предприятиях с использованием синтетических окружений. Эксперименты демонстрируют, что открытие правил во время выполнения делает агентов устойчивыми к смещению данных, в то время как традиционные моделиванием мира деградируют при изменении динамики системы.

en.title: Empowering Agents with Runtime Discovery for Dynamic Enterprise Systems
en.desc: This paper discusses the limitations of traditional world models in machine learning when applied to enterprise systems that have changing dynamics. It introduces enterprise discovery agents that can read system configurations at runtime, allowing them to adapt to tenant-specific business logic. The authors argue that these agents can complement offline training by grounding their predictions in the current system state, making them more robust to changes. They also present CascadeBench, a benchmark for evaluating the performance of these agents in dynamic environments, demonstrating that discovery-based methods outperform fixed models under deployment shifts.

zh.title: 动态环境中的智能发现代理
zh.desc: 本论文探讨了企业发现代理在动态变化的可配置环境中的优势。传统的世界模型依赖于历史数据进行预测，但在企业系统中，业务逻辑常常是特定于租户的，并且会随时间演变。我们提出企业发现代理，通过在运行时读取系统配置来恢复相关的转移动态，而不是仅依赖于内部化的表示。研究表明，在可配置的企业环境中，代理应结合运行时发现机制，以提高对动态变化的适应能力。

papers.title: Teaching Language Models to Think in Code
papers.abstract: Abstract ThinC framework enables mathematical problem solving where code serves as the primary reasoning mechanism instead of a verification tool, demonstrating superior performance on math benchmarks.  AI-generated summary Tool-integrated reasoning (TIR) has emerged as a dominant paradigm for mathematical problem solving in language models, combining natural language (NL) reasoning with code execution. However, this interleaved setup has three key limitations: code often acts as a post-hoc verifier, intermediate NL computations are error-prone, and NL and code play overlapping rather than clearly distinct roles. We propose ThinC (Thinking in Code), a framework in which code itself serves as the reasoner rather than as a tool invoked by NL. A ThinC trajectory begins with a brief NL planning step, after which all reasoning unfolds through code blocks connected only by their execution outputs. We distill 12.2k code-centric trajectories from a teacher model and train ThinC-1.7B and ThinC-4B with supervised fine-tuning followed by reinforcement learning. ThinC-4B consistently outperforms every TIR baseline on five competition-level math benchmarks and even surpasses the much larger Qwen3-235B-A22B-Thinking. Further analysis shows that ThinC reasons through code: 99.2% of its final answers are grounded in interpreter output, and the model recovers reliably from code execution failures without intermediate NL reasoning. Our code and models will be released soon.

ru.title: Код как основной механизм мышления: от инструмента верификации к первичному рассуждению
ru.desc: В статье представлена фреймворк ThinC, которая переосмысляет подход к решению математических задач языковыми моделями. Вместо традиционного подхода, где код используется как инструмент для проверки рассуждений на естественном языке, авторы предлагают сделать код основным механизмом рассуждения. Модель начинает с краткого планирования на естественном языке, а затем всё решение развивается через последовательность блоков кода, связанных только выходами выполнения. Модели ThinC, обученные методом supervised fine-tuning и reinforcement learning, превосходят базовые подходы на конкурсных математических бенчмарках и демонстрируют надёжность при восстановлении после ошибок выполнения кода.

en.title: Think in Code: Revolutionizing Math Problem Solving
en.desc: The ThinC framework revolutionizes mathematical problem solving by using code as the main reasoning tool instead of just a verification method. This approach addresses limitations in traditional Tool-integrated reasoning (TIR) by allowing code to directly execute reasoning processes, minimizing errors associated with natural language computations. ThinC employs a structured method where reasoning is conducted through interconnected code blocks, enhancing clarity and efficiency. The results show that ThinC-4B outperforms existing models on competitive math benchmarks, demonstrating its effectiveness in reasoning through code execution.

zh.title: ThinC：让代码成为推理的核心
zh.desc: ThinC框架使得数学问题解决中，代码成为主要的推理机制，而不是验证工具。与传统的工具集成推理方法相比，ThinC通过代码块的执行输出进行推理，避免了自然语言计算中的错误。我们从教师模型中提取了12.2k个以代码为中心的推理轨迹，并通过监督微调和强化学习训练了ThinC-1.7B和ThinC-4B模型。实验结果表明，ThinC-4B在多个数学基准测试中表现优异，且其推理结果99.2%基于解释器输出，能够有效应对代码执行失败。

papers.title: MCP-Cosmos: World Model-Augmented Agents for Complex Task Execution in MCP Environments
papers.abstract: Abstract MCP-Cosmos integrates generative World Models into the Model Context Protocol ecosystem to enhance agent planning and execution through predictive simulation in latent space.  AI-generated summary The Model Context Protocol (MCP) has unified the interface between Large Language Models (LLMs) and external tools, yet a fundamental gap remains in how agents conceptualize the environments within which they operate. Current paradigms are bifurcated: Task-level planning often ignores execution-time dynamics, while reactive execution lacks long-horizon foresight. We present MCP-Cosmos, a framework that infuses generative World Models (WM) into the MCP ecosystem to enable predictive task automation. By unifying three disparate technologies, namely MCP, World Model, and Agent, we demonstrate that a "Bring Your Own World Model" (BYOWM) strategy allows agents to simulate state transitions and refine plans in a latent space before execution. We conducted experiments using two strategies, namely ReAct and SPIRAL with 2 planning models and 3 representative world models over 20+ MCP-Bench tasks. We observed improvements in Agent's environment interaction KPI such as tool success rate and tool parameter accuracy. The framework also offers new metrics such as Execution Quality to generate new insights about the effectiveness of world models compared to baseline.

ru.title: Агенты, видящие будущее: предсказательное планирование через мировые модели
ru.desc: MCP-Cosmos интегрирует генеративные мировые модели в экосистему Model Context Protocol, позволяя агентам предсказывать последствия действий и планировать поведение в скрытом пространстве представлений. Фреймворк объединяет три технологии: MCP для унификации интерфейсов, мировые модели для симуляции переходов состояний и агентов для принятия решений. Авторы демонстрируют, что подход "принеси свою мировую модель" (BYOWM) улучшает качество взаимодействия агента с окружением, повышая точность выбора инструментов и успешность их применения. Экспериментальная оценка на более чем 20 задачах показала преимущества предложенного подхода и введение новых метрик для оценки эффективности мировых моделей.

en.title: Empowering Agents with Predictive World Models
en.desc: MCP-Cosmos is a framework that enhances agent planning and execution by integrating generative World Models into the Model Context Protocol (MCP) ecosystem. This integration allows agents to simulate their environments in a latent space, improving their ability to plan tasks with foresight while considering execution dynamics. By adopting a 'Bring Your Own World Model' strategy, agents can refine their plans before actual execution, leading to better performance in various tasks. Experiments showed that this approach improved key performance indicators like tool success rate and introduced new metrics for evaluating execution quality.

zh.title: 智能体规划与执行的新纪元
zh.desc: MCP-Cosmos 是一个将生成性世界模型整合到模型上下文协议（MCP）生态系统中的框架，旨在通过潜在空间中的预测模拟来增强智能体的规划和执行能力。该框架解决了当前任务级规划与执行时动态之间的差距，使得智能体能够在执行前模拟状态转变并优化计划。通过结合 MCP、世界模型和智能体三种技术，MCP-Cosmos 采用了“自带世界模型”（BYOWM）策略，提升了智能体与环境交互的效果。实验结果显示，使用 MCP-Cosmos 后，智能体在工具成功率和参数准确性等关键绩效指标上都有显著改善。

papers.title: SeePhys Pro: Diagnosing Modality Transfer and Blind-Training Effects in Multimodal RLVR for Physics Reasoning
papers.abstract: Abstract SeePhys Pro benchmark reveals that current multimodal models struggle with representation-invariant reasoning when information shifts from text to visual formats, and demonstrates that blind training can improve performance through residual textual cues.  AI-generated summary We introduce SeePhys Pro, a fine-grained modality transfer benchmark that studies whether models preserve the same reasoning capability when critical information is progressively transferred from text to image. Unlike standard vision-essential benchmarks that evaluate a single input form, SeePhys Pro features four semantically aligned variants for each problem with progressively increasing visual elements. Our evaluation shows that current frontier models are far from representation-invariant reasoners: performance degrades on average as information moves from language to diagrams, with visual variable grounding as the most critical bottleneck. Motivated by this inference-time fragility, we further develop large training corpora for multimodal RLVR and use blind training as a diagnostic control, finding that RL with all training images masked can still improve performance on unmasked validation sets. To analyze this effect, text-deletion, image-mask-rate, and format-saturation controls suggest that such gains can arise from residual textual and distributional cues rather than valid visual evidence. Our results highlight the need to evaluate multimodal reasoning not only by final-answer accuracy, but also by robustness under modality transfer and by diagnostics that test whether improvements rely on task-critical visual evidence.

ru.title: Мультимодальные модели не инвариантны к смене представления информации
ru.desc: В статье представлен бенчмарк SeePhys Pro, который выявляет, что современные мультимодальные модели теряют способность к рассуждению, когда информация переходит из текстового формата в визуальный. Исследование показывает критическую проблему с привязкой переменных к визуальным элементам и нестабильность моделей при изменении представления данных. Авторы обнаружили, что обучение с маскированными изображениями может улучшать производительность на немаскированных данных благодаря остаточным текстовым подсказкам, а не реальному пониманию визуального содержания. Работа подчеркивает необходимость оценивать мультимодальные рассуждения не только по точности финального ответа, но и по робастности при смене модальностей.

en.title: Enhancing Multimodal Reasoning Through Residual Cues
en.desc: The SeePhys Pro benchmark investigates how well multimodal models can maintain reasoning abilities when information is shifted from text to visual formats. It reveals that current models struggle with this representation-invariant reasoning, particularly when transitioning from language to images, leading to performance drops. The study introduces blind training techniques that utilize masked training images, which surprisingly enhance performance on unmasked validation sets. This suggests that improvements may stem from residual textual cues rather than valid visual information, emphasizing the importance of robust evaluation in multimodal reasoning tasks.

zh.title: 多模态推理的挑战与机遇
zh.desc: 本论文介绍了SeePhys Pro基准测试，旨在研究多模态模型在信息从文本转移到视觉格式时的推理能力。研究发现，当前的多模态模型在处理这种信息转移时表现不佳，尤其是在视觉变量的基础上。通过盲训练，模型在没有视觉信息的情况下仍能提高性能，表明文本线索在推理中起到了重要作用。我们的结果强调了在评估多模态推理时，不仅要关注最终答案的准确性，还要考虑在模态转移下的鲁棒性。

papers.title: World Model for Robot Learning: A Comprehensive Survey
papers.abstract: Abstract World models as predictive representations of environmental dynamics have become essential for robot learning, supporting policy learning, planning, and simulation across various embodied applications.  AI-generated summary World models, which are predictive representations of how environments evolve under actions, have become a central component of robot learning. They support policy learning, planning, simulation, evaluation, data generation, and have advanced rapidly with the rise of foundation models and large-scale video generation. However, the literature remains fragmented across architectures, functional roles, and embodied application domains. To address this gap, we present a comprehensive review of world models from a robot-learning perspective. We examine how world models are coupled with robot policies, how they serve as learned simulators for reinforcement learning and evaluation, and how robotic video world models have progressed from imagination-based generation to controllable, structured, and foundation-scale formulations. We further connect these ideas to navigation and autonomous driving, and summarize representative datasets, benchmarks, and evaluation protocols. Overall, this survey systematically reviews the rapidly growing literature on world models for robot learning, clarifies key paradigms and applications, and highlights major challenges and future directions for predictive modeling in embodied agents. To facilitate continued access to newly emerging works, benchmarks, and resources, we will maintain and regularly update the accompanying GitHub repository alongside this survey.

ru.title: World models как основа предсказательного обучения роботов
ru.desc: В статье представлен полный обзор world models (мировых моделей) — предсказательных представлений динамики окружающей среды, которые стали критически важны для обучения роботов. Авторы рассматривают, как эти модели интегрируются с политиками роботов, используются в качестве learned simulators для reinforcement learning и оценки политик, а также как видео-модели мира эволюционировали от простой генерации образов до структурированных и масштабируемых формулировок. Обзор охватывает применение в навигации и автономном вождении, а также описывает ключевые датасеты, бенчмарки и протоколы оценки. Исследование систематизирует быстро растущую литературу о world models и выделяет основные парадигмы, приложения и направления развития предсказательного моделирования для воплощённых агентов.

en.title: Unifying World Models for Enhanced Robot Learning
en.desc: This paper reviews the concept of world models, which are predictive tools that help robots understand and interact with their environments. It discusses how these models are used in robot learning for tasks like planning and policy learning, and how they have evolved with advancements in AI. The authors highlight the need for a unified understanding of different architectures and applications of world models in robotics. They also provide insights into future challenges and directions for improving predictive modeling in robots, along with resources for ongoing research.

zh.title: 世界模型：机器人学习的核心
zh.desc: 本论文综述了世界模型在机器人学习中的应用，强调其作为环境动态预测表示的重要性。世界模型支持策略学习、规划、模拟和评估，随着基础模型和大规模视频生成的兴起而迅速发展。我们探讨了世界模型与机器人策略的结合，以及它们在强化学习中的作用，特别是在导航和自动驾驶中的应用。最后，论文总结了相关数据集、基准和评估协议，并指出了未来在预测建模方面的挑战和方向。

papers.title: AlphaGRPO: Unlocking Self-Reflective Multimodal Generation in UMMs via Decompositional Verifiable Reward
papers.abstract: Abstract AlphaGRPO enhances multimodal generation by applying Group Relative Policy Optimization to AR-Diffusion Unified Multimodal Models through self-reflective refinement and decompositional verifiable reward mechanisms.  AI-generated summary In this paper, we propose AlphaGRPO, a novel framework that applies Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) to AR-Diffusion Unified Multimodal Models (UMMs) to enhance multimodal generation capabilities without an additional cold-start stage. Our approach unlocks the model's intrinsic potential to perform advanced reasoning tasks: Reasoning Text-to-Image Generation, where the model actively infers implicit user intents, and Self-Reflective Refinement, where it autonomously diagnoses and corrects misalignments in generated outputs. To address the challenge of providing stable supervision for real-world multimodal generation, we introduce the Decompositional Verifiable Reward (DVReward). Unlike holistic scalar rewards, DVReward utilizes an LLM to decompose complex user requests into atomic, verifiable semantic and quality questions, which are then evaluated by a general MLLM to provide reliable and interpretable feedback. Extensive experiments demonstrate that AlphaGRPO yields robust improvements across multimodal generation benchmarks, including GenEval, TIIF-Bench, DPG-Bench and WISE, while also achieving significant gains in editing tasks on GEdit without training on editing tasks. These results validate that our self-reflective reinforcement approach effectively leverages inherent understanding to guide high-fidelity generation. Project page: https://huangrh99.github.io/AlphaGRPO/

ru.title: Самосовершенствующаяся генерация: усиленное обучение для многомодальных моделей
ru.desc: AlphaGRPO — это инновационный метод обучения многомодальных моделей генерации на базе AR-Diffusion, использующий оптимизацию политики относительно группы (GRPO) для улучшения качества генерации изображений и текста. Ключевой вклад заключается в применении самоанализирующего механизма очистки, когда модель автономно диагностирует и исправляет ошибки в своих выходах. Для обеспечения надежной обратной связи авторы предложили декомпозиционную проверяемую награду (DVReward), которая разбивает сложные запросы на атомарные вопросы через LLM и оценивает их мультимодальной моделью. Эксперименты показывают значительные улучшения на множестве бенчмарков и даже обобщение на задачи редактирования без специального обучения.

en.title: Unlocking Multimodal Potential with AlphaGRPO
en.desc: AlphaGRPO is a new framework that improves multimodal generation by using Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) with AR-Diffusion Unified Multimodal Models (UMMs). It enhances the model's ability to generate content by allowing it to reason about user intentions and correct its own mistakes through self-reflective refinement. The framework introduces a novel reward system called Decompositional Verifiable Reward (DVReward), which breaks down complex user requests into simpler questions for better evaluation. Experiments show that AlphaGRPO significantly improves performance on various multimodal benchmarks and editing tasks without needing additional training for those tasks.

zh.title: AlphaGRPO：提升多模态生成的创新框架
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种新框架AlphaGRPO，通过将群体相对策略优化（GRPO）应用于AR-扩散统一多模态模型（UMMs），增强多模态生成能力。该方法无需额外的冷启动阶段，能够激发模型进行高级推理任务，如文本到图像生成和自我反思优化。为了解决现实世界多模态生成的稳定监督问题，我们引入了可分解可验证奖励（DVReward），利用大型语言模型（LLM）将复杂用户请求分解为可验证的语义和质量问题。实验结果表明，AlphaGRPO在多个多模态生成基准测试中表现出显著的改进，验证了其自我反思强化学习方法的有效性。

papers.title: LoopUS: Recasting Pretrained LLMs into Looped Latent Refinement Models
papers.abstract: Abstract LoopUS is a post-training framework that transforms pretrained LLMs into looped architectures for improved reasoning performance through latent-refinement and adaptive early exiting mechanisms.  AI-generated summary Looped computation shows promise in improving the reasoning-oriented performance of LLMs by scaling test-time compute. However, existing approaches typically require either training recurrent models from scratch or applying disruptive retrofits, which involve substantial computational costs and may compromise pretrained capabilities. To address these limitations, we introduce Looped Depth Up-Scaling (LoopUS), a post-training framework that converts a standard pretrained LLM into a looped architecture. As a key technical contribution, LoopUS recasts the pretrained LLM into an encoder, a looped reasoning block, and a decoder. It operationalizes this latent-refinement architecture through four core components: (1) block decomposition, guided by staged representation dynamics; (2) an input-dependent selective gate to mitigate hidden-state drift; (3) random deep supervision for memory-efficient learning over long recursive horizons; and (4) a confidence head for adaptive early exiting. Collectively, these mechanisms transform a standard non-looped model into a looped form while stabilizing it against both computational bottlenecks and representation collapse. Through stable latent looping, LoopUS improves reasoning-oriented performance without extending the generated traces or requiring recurrent training from scratch. For more details, see https://thrillcrazyer.github.io/LoopUS

ru.title: Циклическое уточнение для улучшения логических способностей языковых моделей
ru.desc: LoopUS — это фреймворк для постобучения, который преобразует предобученные языковые модели в циклические архитектуры с механизмом уточнения скрытых представлений. Подход использует четыре ключевых компонента: декомпозицию блоков, селективный вентиль для стабилизации скрытых состояний, глубокий супервизор для эффективного обучения и адаптивный ранний выход на основе доверия. Фреймворк позволяет улучшить логические способности модели во время инференции, масштабируя вычислительные ресурсы, без необходимости переобучения с нуля или разрушительных модификаций архитектуры. LoopUS стабилизирует циклические вычисления и предотвращает коллапс представлений, достигая лучшей производительности на задачах рассуждения без расширения трассировки вывода.

en.title: Transforming LLMs for Enhanced Reasoning with LoopUS
en.desc: LoopUS is a framework that enhances pretrained large language models (LLMs) by converting them into looped architectures, which improves their reasoning abilities. It achieves this through a process called latent-refinement and includes mechanisms for adaptive early exiting, allowing the model to make decisions about when to stop processing. The framework consists of four main components that help maintain the model's performance while reducing computational costs. Overall, LoopUS enables better reasoning without the need for extensive retraining or compromising the model's existing capabilities.

zh.title: LoopUS：提升推理性能的循环架构
zh.desc: LoopUS 是一个后训练框架，旨在将预训练的大型语言模型（LLM）转变为循环架构，以提高推理性能。该框架通过潜在细化和自适应提前退出机制来实现这一目标，避免了从头训练递归模型的高计算成本。LoopUS 将预训练的 LLM 重新构建为编码器、循环推理块和解码器，并通过四个核心组件来优化其性能。最终，LoopUS 在不增加计算负担的情况下，提升了模型的推理能力。

papers.title: From Web to Pixels: Bringing Agentic Search into Visual Perception
papers.abstract: Abstract Researchers introduce WebEye, a benchmark for object localization requiring external knowledge resolution, and Pixel-Searcher, an agent-based approach that connects hidden target identities to visual annotations through search and reasoning.  AI-generated summary Visual perception connects high-level semantic understanding to pixel-level perception, but most existing settings assume that the decisive evidence for identifying a target is already in the image or frozen model knowledge. We study a more practical yet harder open-world case where a visible object must first be resolved from external facts, recent events, long-tail entities, or multi-hop relations before it can be localized. We formalize this challenge as Perception Deep Research and introduce WebEye, an object-anchored benchmark with verifiable evidence, knowledge-intensive queries, precise box/mask annotations, and three task views: Search-based Grounding, Search-based Segmentation, and Search-based VQA. WebEyes contains 120 images, 473 annotated object instances, 645 unique QA pairs, and 1,927 task samples. We further propose Pixel-Searcher, an agentic search-to-pixel workflow that resolves hidden target identities and binds them to boxes, masks, or grounded answers. Experiments show that Pixel-Searcher achieves the strongest open-source performance across all three task views, while failures mainly arise from evidence acquisition, identity resolution, and visual instance binding.

ru.title: От текста к пикселям: агентный поиск для локализации неоднозначных объектов
ru.desc: В статье представлен новый бенчмарк WebEye для локализации объектов, требующий разрешения внешних знаний, и агентный подход Pixel-Searcher, который связывает скрытые идентичности объектов с визуальными аннотациями через поиск и рассуждение. Задача формализуется как Perception Deep Research, где видимый объект сначала должен быть идентифицирован через внешние факты, события, редкие сущности или многошаговые отношения перед локализацией. Предложенный агентный рабочий процесс использует поиск информации для разрешения идентичности целевого объекта и привязки его к боксам, маскам или ответам на вопросы. Эксперименты показывают, что Pixel-Searcher достигает лучших результатов среди открытых решений, при этом основные ошибки возникают при получении доказательств, разрешении идентичности и визуальной привязке объектов.

en.title: WebEye: Bridging Knowledge and Visual Localization
en.desc: This paper presents WebEye, a new benchmark designed for object localization that requires external knowledge to identify targets. It introduces Pixel-Searcher, an innovative approach that links hidden object identities to visual data through a search and reasoning process. The study addresses the challenge of recognizing objects in an open-world context, where information must be gathered from external sources before localization can occur. The authors provide a comprehensive dataset with various tasks and demonstrate that Pixel-Searcher outperforms existing methods, although challenges remain in acquiring evidence and resolving identities.

zh.title: WebEye：连接视觉与外部知识的物体定位新基准
zh.desc: 本研究介绍了WebEye，这是一个需要外部知识解析的物体定位基准。我们提出了Pixel-Searcher，这是一种基于代理的方法，通过搜索和推理将隐藏的目标身份与视觉注释连接起来。与传统方法不同，WebEye关注在开放世界场景中，如何从外部事实和多跳关系中解析可见物体。实验表明，Pixel-Searcher在所有任务视图中表现最佳，但主要的失败来自于证据获取和身份解析。

papers.title: Beyond GRPO and On-Policy Distillation: An Empirical Sparse-to-Dense Reward Principle for Language-Model Post-Training
papers.abstract: Abstract Training efficiency is improved by strategically allocating scarce labeled data through staged reinforcement learning and dense supervision, using sparse rewards for teacher model discovery and dense rewards for student model compression.  AI-generated summary In settings where labeled verifiable training data is the binding constraint, each checked example should be allocated carefully. The standard practice is to use this data directly on the model that will be deployed, for example by running GRPO on the deployment student. We argue that this is often an inefficient allocation because it overlooks a reward-density principle: sparse sequence-level reward should train models where exploration is productive, while dense token-level teacher reward should be used where the aim is to compress behavior into a smaller model. In this view, GRPO-style sparse RL and OPD-style dense teacher supervision are not separate recipes; they are different reward-density regimes. The allocation rule is simple: use scarce labeled training data upstream on the strongest model that can turn it into reward-shaped behavior, then transfer that behavior downstream as dense supervision. We evaluate this rule on verifiable math with Qwen3 and Llama models. At fixed Qwen3-1.7B deployment-student size, an RL-improved 8B teacher distilled through the dense bridge outperforms direct GRPO on the same student, while transfer from the same teacher before RL underperforms. The bridge is important: a forward-KL warmup on teacher rollouts followed by OPD on student rollouts is consistently strongest on MATH before any post-bridge student-side sparse RL, and also gives the best pre-Stage~3 AIME endpoints for the canonical 8B/14B teachers. The bridge also makes later student-side sparse RL effective: GRPO that is weak on a cold student lifts MATH from 75.4% to 78.5% after the bridge and outperforms a matched replay control by 2.8 points. The operational principal is to avoid using scarce labeled data on the least prepared policy: use sparse reward for teacher-side discovery, dense transfer for student compression, and student-side sparse reward only after the bridge.

ru.title: От учителя к студенту: оптимальный путь редких данных через плотный трансфер
ru.desc: В работе предлагается новый подход к эффективному использованию ограниченных размеченных данных при обучении моделей глубокого обучения. Авторы вводят принцип распределения плотности вознаграждений: разреженное вознаграждение следует применять для обучения учительской модели через обучение с подкреплением, а плотное вознаграждение — для компрессии её поведения в меньшую студентскую модель через дистилляцию. Экспериментальные результаты на математических задачах показывают, что двухэтапная схема с промежуточным мостом для трансфера поведения значительно эффективнее прямого применения редкого RL к студентской модели. Данный подход демонстрирует улучшения в точности на бенчмарках MATH и AIME при использовании одного и того же объёма размеченных данных.

en.title: Maximizing Training Efficiency with Smart Data Allocation
en.desc: This paper discusses how to improve training efficiency in machine learning by using labeled data more effectively. It introduces a method that combines staged reinforcement learning with dense supervision, where sparse rewards help discover teacher models and dense rewards assist in compressing student models. The authors argue that using labeled data directly on deployment models is often inefficient and propose a strategy to allocate this data to maximize learning. Their experiments show that this approach leads to better performance in tasks like verifiable math, demonstrating the importance of a structured allocation of training resources.

zh.title: 优化稀缺标记数据的训练效率
zh.desc: 本论文提出了一种通过分阶段强化学习和密集监督来提高训练效率的方法，特别是在标记数据稀缺的情况下。我们强调了奖励密度原则，即稀疏的序列级奖励应用于探索性训练，而密集的标记级奖励则用于模型压缩。通过将稀缺的标记数据分配给最强的模型进行奖励行为的生成，然后将这种行为转移到下游模型进行密集监督，可以有效提高模型性能。实验结果表明，经过这种桥接策略后，学生模型的表现显著提升。

papers.title: Continual Harness: Online Adaptation for Self-Improving Foundation Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract A self-improving AI system for embodied agents autonomously refines its own prompts, skills, and memory through continuous learning without environment resets, achieving human-level performance in complex video games.  AI-generated summary Coding harnesses such as Claude Code and OpenHands wrap foundation models with tools, memory, and planning, but no equivalent exists for embodied agents' long-horizon partial-observability decision-making. We first report our Gemini Plays Pokemon (GPP) experiments. With iterative human-in-the-loop harness refinement, GPP became the first AI system to complete Pokemon Blue, Yellow Legacy on hard mode, and Crystal without a lost battle. In the hardest stages, the agent itself began iterating on its strategy through long-context memory, surfacing emergent self-improvement signals alongside human-in-the-loop refinement. Continual Harness removes the human fully from this loop: a reset-free self-improving harness for embodied agents that formalizes and automates what we observed. Starting from only a minimal environment interface, the agent alternates between acting and refining its own prompt, sub-agents, skills, and memory, drawing on any past trajectory data. Prompt-optimization methods require episode resets; Continual Harness adapts online within a single run. On Pokemon Red and Emerald across frontier models, Continual Harness starting from scratch substantially reduces button-press cost relative to the minimalist baseline and recovers a majority of the gap to a hand-engineered expert harness, with capability-dependent gains, despite starting from the same raw interface with no curated knowledge, no hand-crafted tools, and no domain scaffolding. We then close the loop with the model itself: an online process-reward co-learning loop, in which an open-source agent's rollouts through the refining harness are relabeled by a frontier teacher and used to update the model, drives sustained in-game milestone progress on Pokemon Red without resetting the environment between training iterations.

ru.title: Агент, который учится улучшать сам себя во время игры
ru.desc: Работа представляет систему самосовершенствования для воплощённых агентов, которые автономно улучшают свои собственные подсказки, навыки и память через непрерывное обучение без перезагрузки окружения. Авторы разработали Continual Harness — платформу, которая позволяет агенту итеративно оптимизировать свои стратегии прямо во время взаимодействия с окружением, основываясь на истории своих действий. Система была протестирована на видеоиграх Pokemon, где агент начиная с минимального интерфейса достиг результатов, близких к ручной оптимизации экспертов. Авторы также внедрили цикл обучения с использованием награды за процесс, позволяя пограничным моделям обновляться во время игры без перезагрузок окружения.

en.title: Self-Improving AI: Mastering Games Without Resets
en.desc: This paper presents a self-improving AI system designed for embodied agents that enhances its own capabilities through continuous learning without needing to reset the environment. The system, called Continual Harness, allows the agent to autonomously refine its prompts, skills, and memory while playing complex video games like Pokemon. By utilizing long-context memory and iterative human-in-the-loop refinement, the agent achieves human-level performance and can adapt its strategies in real-time. The approach significantly reduces the need for manual intervention and optimizes performance by learning from past experiences during a single run.

zh.title: 自我改进的AI：无缝学习与优化
zh.desc: 这篇论文介绍了一种自我改进的人工智能系统，能够在复杂的视频游戏中实现人类水平的表现。该系统通过持续学习，自动优化自己的提示、技能和记忆，而无需重置环境。研究中，AI在《口袋妖怪》游戏中表现出色，首次在困难模式下完成了多个版本的游戏，且没有输掉一场战斗。最终，论文提出了一种名为“持续工具”的方法，使得AI能够在单次运行中在线适应和改进，而不需要人工干预。

papers.title: Agent-ValueBench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Evaluating Agent Values
papers.abstract: Abstract Autonomous agents exhibit distinct value systems from underlying language models, requiring new benchmarking approaches to assess alignment across diverse execution environments.  AI-generated summary Autonomous agents have rapidly matured as task executors and seen widespread deployment via harnesses such as OpenClaw. Safety concerns have rightly drawn growing research attention, and beneath them lie the values silently steering agent behavior. Existing value benchmarks, however, remain confined to LLMs, leaving agent values largely uncharted. From intuitive, empirical, and theoretical vantage points, we show that an agent's values diverge from those of its underlying LLM, and the agentic modality further introduces dataset-, evaluation-, and system-level challenges absent from text-only protocols. We close this gap with Agent-ValueBench, the first benchmark dedicated to agent values. It features 394 executable environments across 16 domains, offering 4,335 value-conflict tasks that cover 28 value systems and 332 dimensions. Every instance is co-synthesized through our purpose-built end-to-end pipeline and curated per-instance by professional psychologists. Each task ships with two pole-aligned golden trajectories whose checkpoints anchor a trajectory-level rubric-based judge. Benchmarking 14 frontier proprietary and open-weights models across 4 mainstream harnesses, we uncover three concerted findings. Agent values first manifest as a Value Tide of cross-model homogeneity beneath interpretable counter-currents. This tide bends non-additively under harness pull, and yet more decisively under deliberate steering via embedded skills. Together these results signal that the agent-alignment lever is shifting from classical model alignment and prompt steering toward harness alignment and skill steering.
affiliations: Key Laboratory of Machine Perception (Ministry of Education), Peking University
School of Intelligence Science and Technology, Peking University
School of Psychological and Cognitive Sciences, Peking University
School of Software and Microelectronics, Peking University
State Key Laboratory of General Artificial Intelligence, Peking University

ru.title: От ценностей модели к ценностям агента: выравнивание через архитектуру, а не обучение
ru.desc: В этой работе исследуются ценностные системы автономных агентов, которые значительно отличаются от ценностей базовых языковых моделей. Авторы создали Agent-ValueBench — первый специализированный бенчмарк для оценки ценностей агентов, включающий 394 исполняемых окружения и 4335 задач, охватывающих 28 различных ценностных систем. Они обнаружили, что ценности агентов проявляются как однородный тренд через разные модели, но при этом зависят от выбранной системы выполнения и встроенных навыков. Исследование показывает, что основной рычаг управления согласованностью агентов смещается от выравнивания моделей к выравниванию архитектуры выполнения и развитию специальных навыков.

en.title: Aligning Autonomous Agents: Beyond Language Models
en.desc: This paper discusses the differences between the values of autonomous agents and the underlying language models (LLMs) they are based on. It highlights the need for new benchmarking methods to evaluate how well these agents align with desired values in various environments. The authors introduce Agent-ValueBench, a comprehensive benchmark that includes numerous tasks designed to assess agent values across multiple domains. Their findings suggest that agent values are influenced by the environment and specific skills, indicating a shift in focus from traditional model alignment to harness and skill alignment.

zh.title: 智能体价值的新基准测试
zh.desc: 自主智能体的价值系统与其基础语言模型存在显著差异，因此需要新的基准测试方法来评估在不同执行环境中的一致性。现有的价值基准主要集中在大型语言模型（LLMs）上，导致智能体的价值尚未被充分探索。我们提出了Agent-ValueBench，这是第一个专注于智能体价值的基准，涵盖394个可执行环境和28种价值系统。通过对14个前沿模型的测试，我们发现智能体的价值表现出跨模型的一致性，并且在不同的环境和技能引导下会发生变化。

papers.title: PASA: A Principled Embedding-Space Watermarking Approach for LLM-Generated Text under Semantic-Invariant Attacks
papers.abstract: Abstract PASA is a robust watermarking algorithm for large language models that operates at the semantic level using latent embedding spaces and shared randomness for secure text detection.  AI-generated summary Watermarking for large language models (LLMs) is a promising approach for detecting LLM-generated text and enabling responsible deployment. However, existing watermarking methods are often vulnerable to semantic-invariant attacks, such as paraphrasing. We propose PASA, a principled, robust, and distortion-free watermarking algorithm that embeds and detects a watermark at the semantic level. PASA operates on semantic clusters in a latent embedding space and constructs a distributional dependency between token and auxiliary sequences via shared randomness synchronized by a secret key and semantic history. This design is grounded in our theoretical framework that characterizes a jointly optimal embedding-detection pair, achieving the fundamental trade-offs among detection accuracy, robustness, and distortion. Evaluations across multiple LLMs and semantic-invariant attacks demonstrate that PASA remains robust even under strong paraphrasing attacks while preserving high text quality, outperforming standard vocabulary-space baselines. Ablation studies further validate the effectiveness of our hyperparameter choices. Webpage: https://ai-kunkun.github.io/PASA_page/.
affiliations: The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology (Guangzhou), Guangzhou, China

ru.title: Неуязвимая защита текста: водяные знаки на семантическом уровне
ru.desc: PASA — это надёжный алгоритм водяных знаков для больших языковых моделей, работающий на семантическом уровне в пространстве скрытых векторных представлений. Метод встраивает водяной знак в семантические кластеры текста, используя общую случайность, синхронизируемую секретным ключом и историей. Авторы разработали теоретический фреймворк, характеризующий оптимальную пару встраивания-детекции и обеспечивающий баланс между точностью обнаружения, устойчивостью и качеством текста. Эксперименты показывают, что PASA устойчива к семантически инвариантным атакам типа перефразирования, значительно превосходя базовые методы в словарном пространстве.

en.title: PASA: Robust Watermarking for Secure AI Text Detection
en.desc: PASA is a novel watermarking algorithm designed for large language models (LLMs) that focuses on embedding watermarks at the semantic level. It utilizes latent embedding spaces and shared randomness to ensure secure detection of AI-generated text, making it resilient against common attacks like paraphrasing. The algorithm establishes a distributional dependency between tokens and auxiliary sequences, which is synchronized by a secret key and semantic history. Evaluations show that PASA maintains high detection accuracy and text quality, outperforming traditional methods even under challenging conditions.

zh.title: PASA：语义层面的稳健水印算法
zh.desc: PASA是一种针对大型语言模型的稳健水印算法，能够在语义层面进行操作。它利用潜在嵌入空间和共享随机性来实现安全的文本检测。与现有方法相比，PASA在面对语义不变攻击（如改写）时表现出更强的鲁棒性。该算法通过构建令牌和辅助序列之间的分布依赖关系，确保在保持文本质量的同时实现高效的水印嵌入和检测。

papers.title: MEME: Multi-entity & Evolving Memory Evaluation
papers.abstract: Abstract MEME benchmark evaluates memory systems across multiple entities and evolving conditions, revealing persistent challenges in dependency reasoning despite advanced retrieval and prompting techniques.  AI-generated summary LLM-based agents increasingly operate in persistent environments where they must store, update, and reason over information across many sessions. While prior benchmarks evaluate only single-entity updates, MEME defines six tasks spanning the full space defined by the multi-entity and evolving axes, including three not scored by prior work: Cascade and Absence (dependency reasoning) and Deletion (post-removal state). Evaluating six memory systems spanning three memory paradigms on 100 controlled episodes, we find that all systems collapse on dependency reasoning under the default configuration (Cascade: 3%, Absence: 1% in average accuracy) despite adequate static retrieval performance. Prompt optimization, deeper retrieval, reduced filler noise, and most stronger LLMs fail to close this gap. Only a file-based agent paired with Claude Opus 4.7 as its internal LLM partially closes the gap, but at ~70x the baseline cost, indicating closure currently depends on configurations that are not practical at scale. Code and data are available on the project page: https://seokwonjung-jay.github.io/meme-eval/.
affiliations: Tübingen AI Center, University of Tübingen

ru.title: Память AI-агентов не справляется с логическими зависимостями
ru.desc: В работе представлен бенчмарк MEME для оценки систем памяти AI-агентов, работающих в долгоживущих окружениях с множественными сущностями и изменяющимися условиями. Бенчмарк включает шесть задач, охватывающих различные комбинации многосущностных обновлений и эволюции информации, включая сложные задачи на рассуждение о зависимостях между данными. Исследование показало, что все протестированные системы памяти (охватывающие три парадигмы) критически падают в производительности при рассуждении о зависимостях, даже если они хорошо работают на задачах статического извлечения информации. Авторы обнаружили, что улучшение подсказок, более глубокий поиск и более мощные LLM-модели не решают эту фундаментальную проблему, кроме дорогостоящего решения с Claude Opus, что указывает на необходимость пересмотра подходов к проектированию систем памяти для агентов.

en.title: Evaluating Memory Systems: The Challenge of Dependency Reasoning
en.desc: The MEME benchmark assesses how well memory systems handle information across different entities and changing conditions. It highlights ongoing difficulties in dependency reasoning, even with advanced techniques like retrieval and prompting. The study introduces six tasks that evaluate memory systems in multi-entity scenarios, revealing that all tested systems struggle with dependency reasoning, achieving very low accuracy rates. The findings suggest that while some improvements can be made, they often come at a high cost, making them impractical for widespread use.

zh.title: MEME基准：揭示记忆系统的依赖推理挑战
zh.desc: MEME基准测试评估了在多实体和不断变化的条件下的记忆系统，揭示了尽管有先进的检索和提示技术，依赖推理仍然面临持续挑战。AI生成的基于大语言模型的代理在持久环境中操作，必须在多个会话中存储、更新和推理信息。MEME定义了六个任务，涵盖了多实体和演变轴的完整空间，其中包括三个之前未评分的任务：级联和缺失（依赖推理）以及删除（后移除状态）。在100个受控实验中评估的六个记忆系统显示，所有系统在默认配置下的依赖推理表现不佳，表明当前的解决方案在规模上并不实用。

papers.title: The Many Faces of On-Policy Distillation: Pitfalls, Mechanisms, and Fixes
papers.abstract: Abstract On-policy distillation and self-distillation methods for large language models exhibit varying effectiveness depending on teacher choice, loss formulation, and instance-specific privileged information availability, with identified failure mechanisms including distribution mismatch, optimization instability, and PI-free policy learning.
