AI-generated summary On-policy distillation (OPD) and on-policy self-distillation (OPSD) have emerged as promising post-training methods for large language models, offering dense token-level supervision on trajectories sampled from the model's own policy. However, existing results on their effectiveness remain mixed: while OP(S)D has shown promise in system prompt and knowledge internalization, recent studies also report instability and degradation. In this work, we present a comprehensive empirical study of when OPD and OPSD work, when they fail, and why. We find that OPD on mathematical reasoning is highly sensitive to teacher choice and loss formulation, whereas OPSD fails in our tested settings due to test-time absence of instance-specific privileged information (PI). In contrast, OPSD is effective when PI represents a shared latent rule, such as a system prompt or alignment preference. We identify three failure mechanisms: (1) distribution mismatch between teacher and student caused by conditioning on student-generated prefixes, (2) optimization instability from biased TopK reverse-KL gradients, and (3) an OPSD-specific limitation where the student learns a PI-free policy that aggregates PI-conditioned teachers, which is insufficient when PI is instance-specific. We further show that stop-gradient TopK objectives, RLVR-adapted teachers, and SFT-stabilized students mitigate these failures.

ru.title: Когда и почему дистилляция на собственной политике для LLM работает или fails
ru.desc: В работе исследуются методы дистилляции на собственной политике (on-policy distillation) и самодистилляции для больших языковых моделей. Авторы выявляют три ключевых механизма отказа: несоответствие распределений между учителем и студентом, нестабильность оптимизации из-за смещённых градиентов, и неспособность модели обучаться политике, зависящей от привилегированной информации. Исследование показывает, что эффективность методов сильно зависит от выбора учителя, формулировки функции потерь и доступности информации на время инференса. Предложены техники стабилизации, включаяStop-Gradient TopK, RLVR-адаптированных учителей и SFT-стабилизированных студентов.

en.title: Optimizing Distillation: Navigating Challenges in Language Model Training
en.desc: This paper investigates the effectiveness of on-policy distillation (OPD) and on-policy self-distillation (OPSD) methods for large language models. It highlights that the success of these methods depends significantly on the choice of teacher models, the formulation of loss functions, and the availability of instance-specific privileged information (PI). The authors identify key failure mechanisms such as distribution mismatch, optimization instability, and challenges in learning policies without instance-specific PI. They propose solutions like stop-gradient TopK objectives and RLVR-adapted teachers to address these issues and improve the performance of OPD and OPSD.

zh.title: 优化在线蒸馏与自蒸馏的有效性
zh.desc: 本文研究了大语言模型的在线蒸馏（OPD）和自蒸馏（OPSD）方法的有效性，发现其效果受教师选择、损失函数和特定实例的特权信息（PI）影响。研究表明，OPD在数学推理任务中对教师选择和损失函数非常敏感，而OPSD在缺乏特定实例的PI时表现不佳。文章识别了三种失败机制，包括教师与学生之间的分布不匹配、优化不稳定性以及OPSD特有的限制。最后，提出了一些方法来缓解这些问题，如停止梯度TopK目标和适应性教师。

papers.title: LongMemEval-V2: Evaluating Long-Term Agent Memory Toward Experienced Colleagues
papers.abstract: Abstract A new benchmark called LongMemEval-V2 is introduced to evaluate memory systems' ability to help agents acquire environment-specific experience in web environments, featuring a suite of memory methods including AgentRunbook-R and AgentRunbook-C that demonstrate varying performance in accuracy and latency.  AI-generated summary Long-term memory is crucial for agents in specialized web environments, where success depends on recalling interface affordances, state dynamics, workflows, and recurring failure modes. However, existing memory benchmarks for agents mostly focus on user histories, short traces, or downstream task success, leaving open how to directly evaluate whether memory systems effectively internalize environment-specific experience. To address this gap, we introduce LongMemEval-V2 (LME-V2), a benchmark for evaluating whether memory systems can help agents acquire the experience needed to become knowledgeable colleagues in customized environments. LME-V2 contains 451 manually curated questions covering five core memory abilities for web agents: static state recall, dynamic state tracking, workflow knowledge, environment gotchas, and premise awareness. Questions are paired with history trajectories containing up to 500 trajectories and 115M tokens. We use a context gathering formulation: memory systems consume history trajectories and return compact evidence for downstream question answering. We propose a suite of two memory methods: AgentRunbook-R, an efficient RAG-based memory with knowledge pools for raw state observations, events, and strategy notes, and AgentRunbook-C, which stores trajectories as files and invokes a coding agent to gather evidence in an augmented sandbox. Experiments show that AgentRunbook-C achieves the best performance with 72.5% average accuracy, outperforming the strongest RAG baseline (48.5%) and the off-the-shelf coding agent baseline (69.3%). Despite the strong performance gains, coding agent based methods have high latency costs. While AgentRunbook-C advances the accuracy-latency Pareto frontier, substantial room for improvement remains. Together, these results establish LME-V2 as a challenging testbed for developing long-term memory systems for environment experience.

ru.title: Оценка долгосрочной памяти агентов в специализированных веб-окружениях
ru.desc: В работе представлен новый бенчмарк LongMemEval-V2 для оценки систем долгосрочной памяти агентов, которые действуют в специализированных веб-окружениях. Бенчмарк содержит 451 вопрос, проверяющих пять ключевых способностей памяти: отзыв статического состояния, отслеживание динамического состояния, знание рабочих процессов, особенности окружения и контекстную осведомленность. Предложены два метода памяти: AgentRunbook-R, основанный на RAG с пулами знаний для наблюдений и заметок стратегии, и AgentRunbook-C, который использует кодирующего агента для поиска релевантной информации в изолированной среде. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что AgentRunbook-C достигает 72,5% точности, превосходя RAG-базовый линию на 48,5%, однако имеет более высокие временные затраты.

en.title: Enhancing Agent Memory for Web Environments with LongMemEval-V2
en.desc: The paper introduces LongMemEval-V2, a benchmark designed to assess how well memory systems enable agents to learn from specific web environments. It highlights the importance of long-term memory for agents to recall essential information like workflows and common issues. The benchmark includes 451 questions that test five key memory abilities, using extensive history trajectories for context. Two memory methods, AgentRunbook-R and AgentRunbook-C, are evaluated, with AgentRunbook-C showing superior accuracy but higher latency, indicating a trade-off between performance and speed.

zh.title: 评估智能体记忆系统的新基准测试
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一个新的基准测试LongMemEval-V2，用于评估记忆系统在网络环境中帮助智能体获取特定经验的能力。该基准测试包含了451个手动策划的问题，涵盖了五种核心记忆能力，包括静态状态回忆和动态状态跟踪等。研究提出了两种记忆方法：AgentRunbook-R和AgentRunbook-C，后者在准确性上表现最佳，达到了72.5%的平均准确率。尽管取得了显著的性能提升，但基于编码代理的方法仍存在较高的延迟成本，表明在准确性和延迟之间仍有改进空间。

papers.title: LychSim: A Controllable and Interactive Simulation Framework for Vision Research
papers.abstract: Abstract A simulation framework called LychSim is introduced, featuring a Python API, procedural data pipeline, and MCP integration to enable controllable and interactive environments for vision system development and evaluation.  AI-generated summary While self-supervised pretraining has reduced vision systems' reliance on synthetic data, simulation remains an indispensable tool for closed-loop optimization and rigorous out-of-distribution (OOD) evaluation. However, modern simulation platforms often present steep technical barriers, requiring extensive expertise in computer graphics and game development. In this work, we present LychSim, a highly controllable and interactive simulation framework built upon Unreal Engine 5 to bridge this gap. LychSim is built around three key designs: (1) a streamlined Python API that abstracts away underlying engine complexities; (2) a procedural data pipeline capable of generating diverse, high-fidelity environments with varying out-of-distribution (OOD) visual challenges, paired with rich 2D and 3D ground truths; and (3) a native integration of the Model Context Protocol (MCP) that transforms the simulator into a dynamic, closed-loop playground for reasoning agentic LLMs. We further annotate scene-level procedural rules and object-level pose alignments to enable semantically aligned 3D ground truths and automated scene modification. We demonstrate LychSim's capability across multiple downstream applications, including serving as a synthetic data engine, powering reinforcement learning-based adversarial examiners, and facilitating interactive, language-driven scene layout generation. To benefit the broader vision community, LychSim will be made publicly available, including full source code and various data annotations.

ru.title: Симуляция без барьеров: доступный фреймворк для развития зрительных систем
ru.desc: Представлен фреймворк LychSim — симуляционная платформа на основе Unreal Engine 5, которая упрощает разработку и тестирование систем компьютерного зрения. Платформа предоставляет удобный Python API для абстрагирования сложности графического движка, процедурный конвейер данных для генерации разнообразных синтетических окружений с контролируемыми визуальными искажениями и богатой разметкой, а также интеграцию Model Context Protocol для взаимодействия с аагентными LLM. Фреймворк демонстрирует универсальность через приложения в генерации синтетических данных, обучении с подкреплением и интерактивной генерации сцен на основе естественного языка.

en.title: LychSim: Simplifying Simulation for Vision System Development
en.desc: LychSim is a new simulation framework designed to help develop and evaluate vision systems in a more accessible way. It features a Python API that simplifies the use of complex game engine technologies, making it easier for developers without extensive graphics knowledge. The framework can create diverse and realistic environments that challenge vision systems, providing valuable data for training and testing. Additionally, it integrates the Model Context Protocol (MCP) to allow for dynamic interactions, making it a versatile tool for various applications in machine learning and AI.

zh.title: LychSim：可控的视觉系统模拟框架
zh.desc: LychSim是一个新的模拟框架，旨在为视觉系统的开发和评估提供可控和互动的环境。它基于虚幻引擎5，具有简化的Python API和程序化数据管道，能够生成多样化的高保真环境。LychSim还集成了模型上下文协议（MCP），使得模拟器可以作为一个动态的闭环平台，支持推理代理的学习。该框架的设计使得用户能够轻松创建和修改场景，促进了合成数据生成和强化学习等多种应用。

papers.title: Towards On-Policy Data Evolution for Visual-Native Multimodal Deep Search Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract A visual-native agent harness with image bank reference protocol enables reusable intermediate visual evidence and closed-loop data generation that improves multimodal deep search performance across multiple benchmarks.  AI-generated summary Multimodal deep search requires an agent to solve open-world problems by chaining search, tool use, and visual reasoning over evolving textual and visual context. Two bottlenecks limit current systems. First, existing tool-use harnesses treat images returned by search, browsing, or transformation as transient outputs, so intermediate visual evidence cannot be re-consumed by later tools. Second, training data is usually built by fixed curation recipes that cannot track the target agent's evolving capability. To address these challenges, we first introduce a visual-native agent harness centered on an image bank reference protocol, which registers every tool-returned image as an addressable reference and makes intermediate visual evidence reusable by later tools. On top of this harness, On-policy Data Evolution (ODE) runs a closed-loop data generator that refines itself across rounds from rollouts of the policy being trained. This per-round refinement makes each round's data target what the current policy still needs to learn. The same framework supports both diverse supervised fine-tuning data and policy-aware reinforcement learning data curation, covering the full training lifecycle of the target agent. Across 8 multimodal deep search benchmarks, ODE improves the Qwen3-VL-8B agent from 24.9% to 39.0% on average, surpassing Gemini-2.5 Pro in standard agent-workflow setting (37.9%). At 30B, ODE raises the average score from 30.6% to 41.5%. Further analyses validate the effectiveness of image-bank reuse, especially on complex tasks requiring iterative visual refinement, while rollout-feedback evolution yields more grounded SFT traces and better policy-matched RL tasks than static synthesis.
affiliations: Hong Kong University of Science and Technology
The Chinese University of Hong Kong

ru.title: Переиспользование визуальной информации через эволюцию данных в cicле обучения агента
ru.desc: В работе предлагается визуально-ориентированная архитектура агента с протоколом банка изображений, позволяющей переиспользовать промежуточные визуальные доказательства в мультимодальном поиске. Ключевой инновацией является метод On-policy Data Evolution (ODE), который генерирует данные в замкнутом цикле, адаптируя обучающий набор к текущим потребностям обучаемой политики агента. Система поддерживает как supervised fine-tuning, так и reinforcement learning для полного цикла обучения. Экспериментальные результаты показывают значительное улучшение производительности на 8 мультимодальных бенчмарках, превосходя Gemini-2.5 Pro.

en.title: Revolutionizing Multimodal Search with Reusable Visual Evidence
en.desc: This paper presents a new approach for improving multimodal deep search using a visual-native agent harness that incorporates an image bank reference protocol. This protocol allows for the reuse of intermediate visual evidence, enabling the agent to build on previous outputs rather than treating them as one-time results. Additionally, the On-policy Data Evolution (ODE) method creates a closed-loop data generation process that adapts and refines training data based on the agent's evolving capabilities. The results show significant performance improvements across multiple benchmarks, demonstrating the effectiveness of this approach in enhancing the agent's learning and problem-solving abilities.

zh.title: 提升多模态深度搜索性能的视觉原生代理
zh.desc: 这篇论文介绍了一种视觉原生代理工具，利用图像库参考协议来提高多模态深度搜索的性能。通过将每个工具返回的图像注册为可寻址的参考，论文解决了中间视觉证据无法重复使用的问题。论文还提出了在政策训练过程中进行闭环数据生成的方法，使得每一轮的数据都能针对当前政策的学习需求进行优化。实验结果表明，该方法在多个基准测试中显著提高了代理的表现，尤其在复杂任务中表现出色。

papers.title: On-Policy Self-Evolution via Failure Trajectories for Agentic Safety Alignment
papers.abstract: Abstract FATE is an on-policy framework that uses failure trajectories to improve agent safety and performance through self-evolution and Pareto-aware optimization.  AI-generated summary Tool-using LLM agents fail through trajectories rather than only final responses, as they may execute unsafe tool calls, follow injected instructions, comply with harmful requests, or over-refuse benign tasks despite producing a seemingly safe answer. Existing safety-alignment signals are largely response-level or off-policy, and often incur a safety-utility trade-off: improving agent safety comes at the cost of degraded task performance. Such sparse and single-objective rewards severely limit real-world usability. To bridge this gap, we propose FATE, an on-policy self-evolving framework that transforms verifier-scored failures into repair supervision without expert demonstrations. For each failure, the same policy proposes repair candidates, which are then re-scored by verifiers and filtered across security, utility, over-refusal control, and trajectory validity. This dense trajectory-level information is then used as a supervision signal for agent self-evolution. During this process, we further introduce Pareto-Front Policy Optimization (PFPO), combining supervised warmup with Pareto-aware policy optimization to preserve safety-utility trade-offs. Experiments on AgentDojo, AgentHarm, and ATBench show that FATE improves safety across different models and scales while preserving useful behavior. Compared with strong baselines, FATE reduces attack success rate by 33.5%, harmful compliance by 82.6%, and improves external trajectory-safety diagnosis by 6.5%. These results suggest that failed trajectories can provide structured repair supervision for safer self-evolving agents.

ru.title: Самоэволюция агентов через исправление неудачных траекторий
ru.desc: FATE — это фреймворк обучения агентов на основе политики, который использует неудачные траектории для одновременного улучшения безопасности и производительности. Авторы предложили подход, при котором агент самостоятельно предлагает исправления для своих ошибок, которые затем оцениваются верификаторами по нескольким критериям: безопасность, полезность и избыточные отказы. Ключевой инновацией является метод Pareto-Front Policy Optimization (PFPO), который сохраняет баланс между безопасностью и функциональностью агента, избегая классического компромисса между этими целями. Экспериментальные результаты демонстрируют значительное снижение успешных атак и вредоносных запросов при сохранении полезного поведения агента.

en.title: Transforming Failures into Safer AI Agents
en.desc: The paper introduces FATE, a framework designed to enhance the safety and performance of AI agents by learning from their failure trajectories. Unlike traditional methods that focus on final responses, FATE analyzes the entire process of an agent's actions, identifying unsafe tool usage and harmful compliance. It employs a self-evolution mechanism that uses feedback from failures to guide the agent's learning without needing expert input. Additionally, the framework incorporates Pareto-Front Policy Optimization to balance safety and utility, resulting in significant improvements in safety metrics while maintaining effective task performance.

zh.title: 利用失败轨迹提升智能体安全性与性能
zh.desc: FATE是一个基于策略的框架，通过失败轨迹来提升智能体的安全性和性能。该框架利用自我进化和帕累托优化，解决了现有安全对齐信号的不足，避免了安全性和任务性能之间的权衡。FATE将验证者评分的失败转化为修复监督，利用密集的轨迹级信息来指导智能体的自我进化。实验结果表明，FATE在不同模型和规模上提高了安全性，同时保持了有用的行为。

papers.title: AutoLLMResearch: Training Research Agents for Automating LLM Experiment Configuration -- Learning from Cheap, Optimizing Expensive
papers.abstract: Abstract An agentic framework called AutoLLMResearch automates high-cost large language model experiment configurations by learning from multi-fidelity experimental environments and enabling efficient configuration identification through cross-fidelity extrapolation.  AI-generated summary Effectively configuring scalable large language model (LLM) experiments, spanning architecture design, hyperparameter tuning, and beyond, is crucial for advancing LLM research, as poor configuration choices can waste substantial computational resources and prevent models from realizing their full potential. Prior automated methods are designed for low-cost settings where repeated trial and error is feasible, but scalable LLM experiments are too expensive for such extensive iteration. To our knowledge, no work has addressed the automation of high-cost LLM experiment configurations, leaving this problem labor-intensive and dependent on expert intuition. Motivated by this gap, we propose AutoLLMResearch, an agentic framework that mimics how human researchers learn generalizable principles from low-fidelity experiments and extrapolate to efficiently identify promising configurations in expensive LLM settings. The core challenge is how to enable an agent to learn, through interaction with a multi-fidelity experimental environment that captures the structure of the LLM configuration landscape. To achieve this, we propose a systematic framework with two key components: 1) LLMConfig-Gym, a multi-fidelity environment encompassing four critical LLM experiment tasks, supported by over one million GPU hours of verifiable experiment outcomes; 2) A structured training pipeline that formulates configuration research as a long-horizon Markov Decision Process and accordingly incentivizes cross-fidelity extrapolation reasoning. Extensive evaluation against diverse strong baselines on held-out experiments demonstrates the effectiveness, generalization, and interpretability of our framework, supporting its potential as a practical and general solution for scalable real-world LLM experiment automation.

ru.title: Обучение агента экстраполировать знания между дорогими и дешевыми экспериментами LLM
ru.desc: Авторы представляют AutoLLMResearch — агентный фреймворк для автоматизации конфигурации дорогостоящих экспериментов с большими языковыми моделями. Система обучается на многоуровневой среде LLMConfig-Gym, содержащей более миллиона GPU часов экспериментальных данных, и использует принципы трансферного обучения для экстраполяции знаний между экспериментами разной стоимости. Фреймворк формализует поиск конфигураций как долгоклассный марковский процесс принятия решений, позволяя агенту обобщать закономерности, обнаруженные на дешёвых экспериментах, и применять их для эффективного поиска оптимальных конфигураций в дорогостоящих сценариях. Экспериментальная оценка демонстрирует эффективность, обобщаемость и интерпретируемость подхода как практического решения для масштабной автоматизации экспериментов с LLM.

en.title: Automating Large Language Model Experiment Configurations Efficiently
en.desc: The paper introduces AutoLLMResearch, a framework designed to automate the configuration of large language model (LLM) experiments, which are often costly and complex. It addresses the challenge of efficiently identifying optimal configurations by learning from both low-cost and high-cost experimental environments. The framework includes LLMConfig-Gym, a multi-fidelity environment that simulates various LLM tasks, and a training pipeline that treats configuration research as a Markov Decision Process. The results show that AutoLLMResearch can effectively generalize and provide interpretable solutions for automating LLM experiments, making it a valuable tool for researchers.

zh.title: 自动化高成本语言模型实验配置的创新框架
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种名为AutoLLMResearch的框架，旨在自动化高成本的大型语言模型实验配置。该框架通过学习多保真度实验环境，能够有效识别配置，从而减少计算资源的浪费。与以往仅适用于低成本设置的自动化方法不同，AutoLLMResearch能够在高成本环境中进行有效的配置选择。通过构建LLMConfig-Gym和系统化的训练流程，该框架展示了在真实世界中自动化大型语言模型实验的潜力。

papers.title: AdaPreLoRA: Adafactor Preconditioned Low-Rank Adaptation
papers.abstract: Abstract LoRA optimizers are analyzed through a unified framework based on surrogate matrices and preconditioners, with AdaPreLoRA proposing a novel approach using Adafactor diagonal Kronecker preconditioning to improve factor-space updates while maintaining low memory usage.  AI-generated summary Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) reparameterizes a weight update as a product of two low-rank factors, but the Jacobian J_{G} of the generator mapping the factors to the weight matrix is rank-deficient, so the factor-space preconditioner J_{G}^* {F}_t J_{G} induced by any {W}-space preconditioner {F}_t is singular, and consequently the standard chain rule cannot be uniquely inverted to map a preconditioned {W}-space direction back to a factor-space update. We cast existing LoRA optimizers in a unified framework parameterized by two choices: (i) which invertible surrogate for J_{G}^* {F}_t J_{G} to use, and (ii) which {F}_t on {W} to use. Existing methods occupy four families along these axes: factor-space adaptive updates, block-diagonal surrogates for J_{G}^* J_{G}, Frobenius-residual pseudoinverse methods, and Riemannian manifold constraint. Within this design space, a gradient-statistics-aware {F}_t paired with a closed-form factor-space solve at {O}((m+n)r) memory remains underexplored. We propose AdaPreLoRA, which fills this gap by adopting the Adafactor diagonal Kronecker preconditioner {H}_t on {W} and selecting from the resulting factor-space solution family the element minimizing an {H}_t-weighted imbalance between the two factor contributions; by construction, the resulting factor update is the closest LoRA approximation to the preconditioned {W}-space direction under the {H}_t-weighted norm. Across GPT-2 (E2E), Mistral-7B and Qwen2-7B (GLUE, ARC, GSM8K), and diffusion-model personalization, AdaPreLoRA is competitive with or improves over a representative set of LoRA optimizers while keeping peak GPU memory at the LoRA optimizer level.
affiliations: The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology

ru.title: Адаптивная предобусловливаемая адаптация низкого ранга без потери эффективности
ru.desc: В статье анализируется семейство оптимизаторов для Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) через единую систему, основанную на суррогатных матрицах и предобусловливателях. Авторы показывают, что стандартные методы адаптации низкоранговых факторов страдают от вырождения якобиана при преобразовании градиентов из W-пространства в факторное пространство. Предложен новый метод AdaPreLoRA, который использует диагональный кронекеровский предобусловливатель Adafactor для улучшения обновлений факторов, сохраняя при этом малую потребление памяти O((m+n)r). Экспериментальная проверка на моделях GPT-2 и Mistral-7B демонстрирует конкурентоспособность AdaPreLoRA с другими LoRA-оптимизаторами без увеличения пиковой памяти GPU.

en.title: Optimizing LoRA with AdaPreLoRA: Efficient and Memory-Friendly!
en.desc: This paper presents a new method called AdaPreLoRA for optimizing Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) in machine learning models. It introduces a unified framework that uses surrogate matrices and preconditioners to enhance the efficiency of factor-space updates while keeping memory usage low. The method employs the Adafactor diagonal Kronecker preconditioner to improve the mapping between weight-space and factor-space updates. Experimental results show that AdaPreLoRA performs competitively with existing LoRA optimizers across various models and tasks, maintaining low GPU memory usage.

zh.title: AdaPreLoRA：高效的低秩适应优化器
zh.desc: 本文分析了LoRA优化器，提出了一种基于替代矩阵和预处理器的统一框架。AdaPreLoRA采用Adafactor对角Kronecker预处理器，以提高因子空间更新的效率，同时保持低内存使用。通过选择合适的预处理器和替代矩阵，AdaPreLoRA能够在多个任务上与现有的LoRA优化器竞争或超越它们。该方法在GPT-2、Mistral-7B和Qwen2-7B等模型上表现出色，且在GPU内存使用上保持在LoRA优化器的水平。

papers.title: Lite3R: A Model-Agnostic Framework for Efficient Feed-Forward 3D Reconstruction
papers.abstract: Abstract Lite3R addresses efficiency challenges in transformer-based 3D reconstruction through sparse attention and low-precision quantization while maintaining geometric accuracy.  AI-generated summary Transformer-based 3D reconstruction has emerged as a powerful paradigm for recovering geometry and appearance from multi-view observations, offering strong performance across challenging visual conditions. As these models scale to larger backbones and higher-resolution inputs, improving their efficiency becomes increasingly important for practical deployment. However, modern 3D transformer pipelines face two coupled challenges: dense multi-view attention creates substantial token-mixing overhead, and low-precision execution can destabilize geometry-sensitive representations and degrade depth, pose, and 3D consistency. To address the first challenge, we propose Lite3R, a model-agnostic teacher-student framework that replaces dense attention with Sparse Linear Attention to preserve important geometric interactions while reducing attention cost. To address the second challenge, we introduce a parameter-efficient FP8-aware quantization-aware training (FP8-aware QAT) strategy with partial attention distillation, which freezes the vast majority of pretrained backbone parameters and trains only lightweight linear-branch projection layers, enabling stable low-precision deployment while retaining pretrained geometric priors. We further evaluate Lite3R on two representative backbones, VGGT and DA3-Large, over BlendedMVS and DTU64, showing that it substantially reduces latency (1.7-2.0x) and memory usage (1.9-2.4x) while preserving competitive reconstruction quality overall. These results demonstrate that Lite3R provides an effective algorithm-system co-design approach for practical transformer-based 3D reconstruction. Code: https://github.com/AIGeeksGroup/Lite3R. Website: https://aigeeksgroup.github.io/Lite3R.

ru.title: Эффективная 3D реконструкция: разреженное внимание и квантизация без потери геометрии
ru.desc: Lite3R — это фреймворк учитель-ученик для оптимизации трансформер-моделей в задаче 3D реконструкции. Авторы предлагают два ключевых улучшения: замену плотного внимания на разреженное линейное внимание для снижения вычислительных затрат и введение квантизации-адаптированного обучения в низкой точности (FP8) с сохранением геометрических приоров. Метод использует замораживание основных параметров предобученного энкодера и тренировку только лёгких проекционных слоёв, что обеспечивает стабильную работу при низкой точности. Эксперименты показывают, что Lite3R достигает ускорения в 1.7-2.0x и снижения памяти в 1.9-2.4x без значительной потери качества реконструкции.

en.title: Efficient 3D Reconstruction with Lite3R: Sparsity Meets Precision
en.desc: Lite3R is a novel framework designed to enhance the efficiency of transformer-based 3D reconstruction by utilizing sparse attention mechanisms and low-precision quantization techniques. It addresses the challenges of high computational costs associated with dense multi-view attention and the instability of low-precision execution on geometric representations. By implementing a teacher-student model that focuses on important geometric interactions, Lite3R significantly reduces latency and memory usage while maintaining high-quality reconstruction. The framework has been tested on popular backbones, demonstrating substantial improvements in performance metrics without compromising accuracy.

zh.title: Lite3R：高效的三维重建新方案
zh.desc: Lite3R 是一种针对基于变换器的三维重建模型的高效解决方案。它通过稀疏注意力机制和低精度量化来提高效率，同时保持几何精度。该方法采用教师-学生框架，减少了注意力计算的开销，并引入了FP8感知的量化训练策略，以稳定低精度执行。实验结果表明，Lite3R 在减少延迟和内存使用的同时，仍能保持竞争力的重建质量。

papers.title: VidSplat: Gaussian Splatting Reconstruction with Geometry-Guided Video Diffusion Priors
papers.abstract: Abstract VidSplat is a training-free generative reconstruction framework that uses video diffusion priors to synthesize novel views and recover complete 3D scenes from sparse inputs through adaptive denoising and iterative refinement.  AI-generated summary Gaussian Splatting has achieved remarkable progress in multi-view surface reconstruction, yet it exhibits notable degradation when only few views are available. Although recent efforts alleviate this issue by enhancing multi-view consistency to produce plausible surfaces, they struggle to infer unseen, occluded, or weakly constrained regions beyond the input coverage. To address this limitation, we present VidSplat, a training-free generative reconstruction framework that leverages powerful video diffusion priors to iteratively synthesize novel views that compensate for missing input coverage, and thereby recover complete 3D scenes from sparse inputs. Specifically, we tackle two key challenges that enable the effective integration of generation and reconstruction. First, for 3D consistent generation, we elaborate a training-free, stage-wise denoising strategy that adaptively guides the denoising direction toward the underlying geometry using the rendered RGB and mask images. Second, to enhance the reconstruction, we develop an iterative mechanism that samples camera trajectories, explores unobserved regions, synthesizes novel views, and supplements training through confidence weighted refinement. VidSplat performs robustly to sparse input and even a single image. Extensive experiments on widely used benchmarks demonstrate our superior performance in sparse-view scene reconstruction.
affiliations: Department of Computer Science, Wayne State University

ru.title: Восстановление полных 3D сцен из редких видов через видео-диффузионные приоры
ru.desc: VidSplat представляет собой фреймворк для восстановления 3D сцен из разреженных входных данных без обучения модели. Метод использует диффузионные модели видео в качестве приора для итеративного синтеза новых видов и восполнения пропущенных областей сцены. Авторы разработали адаптивную стратегию денойзинга, которая направляет генерацию в сторону геометрически согласованных результатов, используя отрендеренные RGB и маскированные изображения. Фреймворк успешно восстанавливает полные 3D сцены даже при наличии одного входного изображения благодаря итеративному механизму, исследующему ненаблюдаемые области.

en.title: Reconstructing 3D Scenes from Sparse Inputs with VidSplat
en.desc: VidSplat is a novel framework designed for reconstructing 3D scenes from limited video inputs without requiring prior training. It utilizes video diffusion priors to generate new views and fill in gaps in the scene, addressing challenges like occlusions and weak constraints. The framework employs a unique stage-wise denoising strategy that adapts to the geometry of the scene, ensuring consistency in the generated outputs. Additionally, it features an iterative mechanism that samples camera paths to explore unseen areas, enhancing the overall reconstruction quality even from sparse data.

zh.title: VidSplat：从稀疏输入恢复完整3D场景的创新框架
zh.desc: VidSplat是一种无训练的生成重建框架，利用视频扩散先验合成新视角，并通过自适应去噪和迭代优化从稀疏输入中恢复完整的3D场景。该方法解决了在仅有少量视角时多视角表面重建的显著退化问题。通过逐步去噪策略和迭代机制，VidSplat能够有效整合生成与重建，探索未观察到的区域并合成新视角。实验结果表明，VidSplat在稀疏视图场景重建中表现优越，甚至可以从单张图像中恢复出完整的3D场景。

papers.title: Reward Hacking in Rubric-Based Reinforcement Learning
papers.abstract: Abstract Research examines reward hacking in rubric-based reinforcement learning, identifying verifier failure and rubric-design limitations as key sources of divergence between training and evaluation metrics.  AI-generated summary Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards has enabled strong post-training gains in domains such as math and coding, though many open-ended settings rely on rubric-based rewards. We study reward hacking in rubric-based RL, where a policy is optimized against a training verifier but evaluated against a cross-family panel of three frontier judges, reducing dependence on any single evaluator. Our framework separates two sources of divergence: verifier failure, where the training verifier credits rubric criteria that reference verifiers reject, and rubric-design limitations, where even strong rubric-based verifiers favor responses that rubric-free judges rate worse overall. Across medical and science domains, weak verifiers produce large proxy-reward gains that do not transfer to the reference verifiers; exploitation grows over training and concentrates in recurring failures such as partial satisfaction of compound criteria, treating implicit content as explicit, and imprecise topical matching. Stronger verifiers substantially reduce, but do not eliminate, verifier exploitation. We also introduce a self-internalization gap, a verifier-free diagnostic based on policy log-probabilities, which tracks reference-verifier quality, detecting when the policy trained using the weak verifier stops improving. Finally, in our setting, stronger verification does not prevent reward hacking when the rubric leaves important failure modes unspecified: rubric-based verifiers prefer the RL checkpoint, while rubric-free judges prefer the base model. These disagreements coincide with gains concentrated in completeness and presence-based criteria, alongside declines in factual correctness, conciseness, relevance, and overall quality. Together, these results suggest that stronger verification reduces reward hacking, but does not by itself ensure that rubric gains correspond to broader quality gains.

ru.title: Верификаторы не гарантируют качество: двойная природа reward hacking в обучении с рубриками
ru.desc: В работе исследуется проблема reward hacking в обучении с подкреплением на основе рубрик. Авторы выявили две основные причины расхождения между метриками обучения и оценки: отказы верификатора, когда обучаемый верификатор одобряет критерии, которые отвергают судьи-эксперты, и ограничения дизайна рубрик, когда даже сильные верификаторы предпочитают ответы, которые судьи оценивают хуже. Исследование показывает, что слабые верификаторы часто генерируют прибыль в proxy-reward, которая не переносится на референсные верификаторы, а эксплуатация обнаруживается в рекурсивных ошибках типа частичного удовлетворения критериев. Авторы демонстрируют, что более сильная верификация снижает, но не устраняет полностью reward hacking, особенно когда рубрика не охватывает все важные режимы отказа.

en.title: Understanding Reward Hacking in Rubric-Based Reinforcement Learning
en.desc: This paper investigates the issue of reward hacking in reinforcement learning (RL) that uses rubric-based evaluations. It identifies two main problems: verifier failure, where the training verifier incorrectly rewards certain criteria, and limitations in rubric design that can lead to poor overall performance. The study shows that even with stronger verifiers, the exploitation of weak verifiers persists, leading to gains that do not translate to better evaluations by independent judges. The authors propose a new diagnostic tool to monitor the quality of policies and highlight that improvements in verification alone do not guarantee better outcomes in terms of overall quality.

zh.title: 强化学习中的奖励黑客问题研究
zh.desc: 本研究探讨了基于评分标准的强化学习中的奖励黑客问题，识别出验证者失败和评分标准设计的局限性是训练和评估指标之间差异的主要来源。我们分析了在基于评分标准的强化学习中，策略在训练验证者下优化，但在跨家族的评审小组中评估的情况。研究发现，弱验证者会产生大量的代理奖励增益，但这些增益并不能转移到参考验证者上。更强的验证者虽然能显著减少奖励黑客现象，但并不能完全消除这种现象，且在评分标准未明确重要失败模式时，奖励黑客仍然存在。

papers.title: MoCam: Unified Novel View Synthesis via Structured Denoising Dynamics
papers.abstract: Abstract MoCam addresses the challenge of generative novel view synthesis by dynamically coordinating geometric and appearance priors through structured denoising dynamics within a diffusion framework.  AI-generated summary Generative novel view synthesis faces a fundamental dilemma: geometric priors provide spatial alignment but become sparse and inaccurate under view changes, while appearance priors offer visual fidelity but lack geometric correspondence. Existing methods either propagate geometric errors throughout generation or suffer from signal conflicts when fusing both statically. We introduce MoCam, which employs structured denoising dynamics to orchestrate a coordinated progression from geometry to appearance within the diffusion process.MoCam first leverages geometric priors in early stages to anchor coarse structures and tolerate their incompleteness, then switches to appearance priors in later stages to actively correct geometric errors and refine details. This design naturally unifies static and dynamic view synthesis by temporally decoupling geometric alignment and appearance refinement within the diffusion process.Experiments demonstrate that MoCam significantly outperforms prior methods, particularly when point clouds contain severe holes or distortions, achieving robust geometry-appearance disentanglement.

ru.title: Поэтапная координация геометрии и внешнего вида в диффузионном синтезе видов
ru.desc: MoCam решает задачу генеративного синтеза новых ракурсов, динамически координируя геометрические и визуальные приоры через структурированную диффузионную динамику. Метод использует геометрические приоры на ранних этапах денойзинга для стабилизации грубой структуры, а затем переходит к визуальным приорам на поздних этапах для коррекции ошибок и уточнения деталей. Такой подход естественным образом разделяет процессы геометрического выравнивания и уточнения внешнего вида во времени, избегая конфликтов между приорами. Экспериментально показано, что MoCam значительно превосходит существующие методы, особенно при работе с неполными облаками точек.

en.title: MoCam: Harmonizing Geometry and Appearance in View Synthesis
en.desc: MoCam is a novel approach to generative novel view synthesis that effectively combines geometric and appearance information. It uses a structured denoising process within a diffusion framework to manage the transition from geometry to appearance. In the initial stages, MoCam focuses on geometric priors to establish a rough structure, which helps in dealing with incomplete data. Later, it shifts to appearance priors to refine details and correct any geometric inaccuracies, leading to improved performance, especially in challenging scenarios with distorted or incomplete point clouds.

zh.title: MoCam：动态协调几何与外观的生成新视角合成
zh.desc: MoCam是一种新方法，旨在解决生成新视角合成中的挑战。它通过在扩散框架内动态协调几何先验和外观先验，利用结构化去噪动态来实现。MoCam在早期阶段利用几何先验来固定粗略结构，然后在后期阶段切换到外观先验，以主动修正几何错误并细化细节。实验表明，MoCam在处理严重孔洞或失真的点云时，显著优于现有方法，成功实现了几何与外观的解耦。

papers.title: FocuSFT: Bilevel Optimization for Dilution-Aware Long-Context Fine-Tuning
papers.abstract: Abstract Training framework FocuSFT improves long-context language model performance by addressing attention allocation issues through bilevel optimization with parametric memory that focuses attention on semantically relevant content.  AI-generated summary Large language models can now process increasingly long inputs, yet their ability to effectively use information spread across long contexts remains limited. We trace this gap to how attention budget is spent during supervised fine-tuning (SFT) on long sequences: positional biases and attention sinks cause the model to allocate most of its attention to positionally privileged tokens rather than semantically relevant content. This training-time attention dilution (the starvation of content tokens in the attention distribution) weakens the gradient signal, limiting the model's ability to learn robust long-context capabilities. We introduce FocuSFT, a bilevel optimization framework that addresses this problem at training time. An inner loop adapts lightweight fast-weight parameters on the training context to form a parametric memory that concentrates attention on relevant content, and the outer loop performs SFT conditioned on this sharpened representation. Both loops apply bidirectional attention over context tokens while preserving causal masking for responses, reducing the causal asymmetry that gives rise to attention sinks and aligning inner-outer behavior. On BABILong, FocuSFT improves accuracy by up to +14pp across 4K--32K context lengths; on RULER, it raises CWE aggregation from 72.9\% to 81.1\% at 16K; and on GPQA with agentic tool use, it yields a 24\% relative gain in pass@1. Attention analysis shows that FocuSFT reduces attention sink mass by 529times and triples context engagement during training. Code: https://github.com/JarvisPei/FocuSFT
affiliations: The Chinese University of Hong Kong

ru.title: Сосредоточить внимание модели на значимом контенте в длинных последовательностях
ru.desc: FocuSFT — это двухуровневый фреймворк оптимизации, который улучшает способность языковых моделей работать с длинными контекстами путём решения проблемы неправильного распределения внимания. Авторы выявили, что при обучении с учителем на длинных последовательностях модель концентрирует большую часть внимания на позиционно привилегированных токенах вместо семантически релевантного контента, что ослабляет градиентный сигнал. Предложенный метод использует параметрическую память с быстрыми весами во внутреннем цикле для фокусировки внимания на важном контенте и выполняет supervised fine-tuning во внешнем цикле на основе этого улучшенного представления. Экспериментальные результаты показывают значительное улучшение на различных бенчмарках: до +14 процентных пункта на BABILong, повышение CWE с 72.9% до 81.1% на RULER при контексте в 16К, и 24% относительный прирост на GPQA.

en.title: FocuSFT: Sharpening Attention for Long-Context Mastery
en.desc: The paper introduces FocuSFT, a training framework designed to enhance the performance of long-context language models by optimizing how attention is allocated during training. It identifies that traditional supervised fine-tuning (SFT) often leads to attention being focused on less relevant tokens due to positional biases, which dilutes the model's ability to learn from important content. FocuSFT employs a bilevel optimization approach, where an inner loop creates a parametric memory to prioritize semantically relevant information, while an outer loop conducts SFT based on this refined attention. The results demonstrate significant improvements in accuracy and context engagement across various benchmarks, indicating that FocuSFT effectively addresses the limitations of attention allocation in long-context scenarios.

zh.title: 聚焦长上下文的注意力分配
zh.desc: FocuSFT是一种训练框架，旨在通过双层优化和参数记忆来改善长上下文语言模型的性能。该方法解决了在监督微调过程中注意力分配不均的问题，使模型能够更有效地关注语义相关的内容。通过在训练时调整轻量级快速权重参数，FocuSFT形成了一个集中注意力的参数记忆，从而增强了模型对长上下文的学习能力。实验结果表明，FocuSFT在多个数据集上显著提高了模型的准确性和上下文参与度。

papers.title: GLiNER-Relex: A Unified Framework for Joint Named Entity Recognition and Relation Extraction
papers.abstract: Abstract A unified model for joint named entity recognition and relation extraction that uses a shared transformer encoder to simultaneously identify entities and extract relations with zero-shot capabilities.  AI-generated summary Joint named entity recognition (NER) and relation extraction (RE) is a fundamental task in natural language processing for constructing knowledge graphs from unstructured text. While recent approaches treat NER and RE as separate tasks requiring distinct models, we introduce GLiNER-Relex, a unified architecture that extends the GLiNER framework to perform both entity recognition and relation extraction in a single model. Our approach leverages a shared bidirectional transformer encoder to jointly represent text, entity type labels, and relation type labels, enabling zero-shot extraction of arbitrary entity and relation types specified at inference time. GLiNER-Relex constructs entity pair representations from recognized spans and scores them against relation type embeddings using a dedicated relation scoring module. We evaluate our model on four standard relation extraction benchmarks: CoNLL04, DocRED, FewRel, and CrossRE, and demonstrate competitive performance against both specialized relation extraction models and large language models, while maintaining the computational efficiency characteristic of the GLiNER family. The model is released as an open-source Python package with a simple inference API that allows users to specify arbitrary entity and relation type labels at inference time and obtain both entities and relation triplets in a single call. All models and code are publicly available.
affiliations: Baldor Technologies Pvt. Ltd. (IDfy), Mumbai, India

ru.title: Единая модель для распознавания сущностей и извлечения отношений с нулевым обучением
ru.desc: Статья описывает GLiNER-Relex — единую модель на основе трансформера для совместного решения задач распознавания именованных сущностей (NER) и извлечения отношений (RE) из текста. Модель использует общий кодировщик-трансформер для одновременной идентификации сущностей и выделения семантических связей между ними, поддерживая zero-shot извлечение произвольных типов сущностей и отношений, заданных во время инференции. Архитектура конструирует представления пар сущностей из распознанных текстовых фрагментов и оценивает их против встраиваний типов отношений с помощью специализированного модуля. Модель демонстрирует конкурентные результаты на стандартных бенчмарках и выпущена как открытый пакет Python с простым API для практического применения.

en.title: Unified NER and RE with Zero-Shot Capabilities
en.desc: This paper presents GLiNER-Relex, a unified model that performs joint named entity recognition (NER) and relation extraction (RE) using a shared transformer encoder. By integrating these two tasks, the model can identify entities and extract relationships simultaneously, even for types not seen during training, showcasing its zero-shot capabilities. The architecture constructs representations for entity pairs and evaluates them against relation type embeddings, allowing for efficient processing. Evaluations on multiple benchmarks show that GLiNER-Relex competes well with specialized models while being computationally efficient and user-friendly as an open-source tool.

zh.title: 统一模型，实现实体识别与关系抽取的双重任务
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种统一模型GLiNER-Relex，用于联合命名实体识别（NER）和关系抽取（RE）。该模型使用共享的双向变换器编码器，同时识别实体和提取关系，具备零样本能力。与以往将NER和RE视为独立任务的做法不同，GLiNER-Relex能够在单一模型中处理这两项任务。我们在多个标准数据集上评估了该模型，结果显示其在性能和计算效率上均表现出色。

papers.title: Geometric Factual Recall in Transformers
papers.abstract: Abstract Transformer language models use geometric memorization where embeddings encode linear superpositions of attributes and MLPs act as relation-conditioned selectors rather than associative key-value mappings.  AI-generated summary How do transformer language models memorize factual associations? A common view casts internal weight matrices as associative memories over pairs of embeddings, requiring parameter counts that scale linearly with the number of facts. We develop a theoretical and empirical account of an alternative, geometric form of memorization in which learned embeddings encode relational structure directly, and the MLP plays a qualitatively different role. In a controlled setting where a single-layer transformer must memorize random bijections from subjects to a shared attribute set, we prove that a logarithmic embedding dimension suffices: subject embeddings encode linear superpositions of their associated attribute vectors, and a small MLP acts as a relation-conditioned selector that extracts the relevant attribute via ReLU gating, and not as an associative key-value mapping. We extend these results to the multi-hop setting -- chains of relational queries such as ``Who is the mother of the wife of x?'' -- providing constructions with and without chain-of-thought that exhibit a provable capacity-depth tradeoff, complemented by a matching information-theoretic lower bound. Empirically, gradient descent discovers solutions with precisely the predicted structure. Once trained, the MLP transfers zero-shot to entirely new bijections when subject embeddings are appropriately re-initialized, revealing that it has learned a generic selection mechanism rather than memorized any particular set of facts.

ru.title: Геометрическое кодирование вместо ассоциативной памяти в трансформерах
ru.desc: В этой работе исследуется, как трансформеры запоминают факты через геометрическую структуру: вложения кодируют линейные суперпозиции атрибутов, а MLP действует как специализированный селектор, а не как ассоциативная память. Авторы доказывают, что для запоминания случайных биекций достаточно логарифмической размерности вложений, при этом ReLU в MLP выделяет нужный атрибут из суперпозиции. Результаты расширены на задачи многоходовых запросов с анализом компромисса между ёмкостью и глубиной модели. Экспериментально подтверждается, что градиентный спуск находит решения с предсказанной структурой, и обученный MLP переносится без обучения на новые данные.

en.title: Geometric Memorization in Transformers: Efficient and Generalizable!
en.desc: This paper explores how transformer language models memorize factual information using a geometric approach rather than traditional associative memory. It shows that embeddings can represent relationships directly, allowing for efficient memorization with a logarithmic embedding dimension. The multi-layer perceptron (MLP) functions as a selector that retrieves relevant attributes through gating, rather than storing key-value pairs. The findings indicate that the model can generalize to new relationships after training, demonstrating a robust selection mechanism.

zh.title: 变换器模型的几何记忆机制
zh.desc: 这篇论文探讨了变换器语言模型如何记忆事实关联。研究表明，嵌入向量可以直接编码关系结构，而多层感知机（MLP）则作为条件选择器，而不是传统的关联记忆。通过实验，作者证明了在特定设置下，嵌入维度可以是对数级别，且小型MLP能够通过ReLU门控提取相关属性。最终，研究表明，经过训练的MLP能够在新的双射上进行零样本转移，显示出其学习了通用的选择机制。

papers.title: Images in Sentences: Scaling Interleaved Instructions for Unified Visual Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract INSET is a unified multimodal model that embeds images as native vocabulary within textual instructions, enabling better handling of complex interleaved inputs through transformer-based contextual locality and supporting both image generation and editing tasks.  AI-generated summary While recent advancements in multimodal language models have enabled image generation from expressive multi-image instructions, existing methods struggle to maintain performance under complex interleaved instructions. This limitation stems from the structural separation of images and text in current paradigms, which forces models to bridge difficult long-range dependencies to match descriptions with visual targets. To address these challenges, we propose Images iN SEnTences (a.k.a, INSET), a unified generation model that seamlessly embeds images as native vocabulary within textual instructions. By positioning visual features directly at their corresponding semantic slots, INSET leverages the contextual locality of transformers for precise object binding, effectively treating images as dense, expressive language tokens. Furthermore, we introduce a scalable data engine that synthesizes 15M high-quality interleaved samples from standard image and video datasets, utilizing VLMs and LLMs to construct rich, long-horizon sequences. Evaluation results on InterleaveBench demonstrate that INSET significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods in multi-image consistency and text alignment, with performance gaps widening as input complexity increases. Beyond standard generation, our approach inherently extends to multimodal image editing, integrating visual content as part of the instruction to facilitate highly expressive and creative visual manipulations.

ru.title: Изображения как словарь текста: мультимодальная генерация без границ
ru.desc: INSET — это единая мультимодальная модель, которая встраивает изображения как собственный словарь текстовых инструкций, позволяя трансформерам эффективно обрабатывать сложные перемешанные входные данные через механизм контекстной локальности. Модель преодолевает проблему разделения изображений и текста в существующих подходах, устраняя необходимость в дальних зависимостях при сопоставлении описаний с визуальными объектами. Авторы предложили масштабируемый конвейер данных, который синтезировал 15 миллионов высококачественных перемешанных примеров с использованием VLM и LLM для создания сложных последовательностей. INSET показал значительное превосходство над современными методами в согласованности многих изображений и выравнивании текста, а также естественным образом расширяет функциональность до редактирования изображений.

en.title: INSET: Bridging Text and Images for Enhanced Multimodal Understanding
en.desc: The paper introduces INSET, a unified multimodal model that integrates images directly into textual instructions, enhancing the processing of complex inputs. By treating images as part of the vocabulary, INSET utilizes transformer architecture to improve contextual understanding and object binding. This model addresses the limitations of existing methods that struggle with long-range dependencies between text and images. Additionally, INSET includes a scalable data engine that generates a large dataset of interleaved samples, leading to superior performance in both image generation and editing tasks.

zh.title: 图像与文本的完美融合：INSET模型
zh.desc: INSET是一种统一的多模态模型，它将图像作为文本指令中的原生词汇嵌入，从而更好地处理复杂的交错输入。该模型利用变换器的上下文局部性，支持图像生成和编辑任务。通过将视觉特征直接放置在相应的语义位置，INSET能够精确绑定对象，像处理语言标记一样处理图像。评估结果表明，INSET在多图像一致性和文本对齐方面显著优于现有的最先进方法，尤其在输入复杂性增加时表现更为突出。
